_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-10 15:47:33Z)**_

确认了。根因不是代码,是 **`raw.githubusercontent.com` 的 CDN 缓存**:你那次跑的其实是**修复前的旧 `bootstrap.ps1`**。

三条证据链对上了:

1. **修复已经上线** — `52e7879` 在 `origin/main` 上,本地与远端同步 (`main...origin/main`),而且我刚抓的 `raw` URL 现在返回的就是带 `Test-Admin` 的新版。
2. **新旧代码逻辑上不可能产生你看到的输出** — scoop 自己的 admin 检测和你的 `Test-Admin` 是**逐字相同**的一行 `IsInRole(...Administrator)`。同一个进程、同一个 token,`IsInRole` 是确定性的,两者**不可能**一个说"不是管理员"(走 else 分支打印 `==> Installing scoop`)、另一个说"是管理员"而 abort。这个矛盾只能说明:跑的根本不是修复后的脚本。
3. **旧版本完美匹配你的输出** — 修复前的父提交 `b25b8d4` 那段是:
   ```powershell
   Info 'Installing scoop'                       # → 打印 "==> Installing scoop"
   Invoke-RestMethod get.scoop.sh | Invoke-Expression   # 没有 -RunAsAdmin,elevated 下必 abort
   ```
   没有 `Test-Admin`、没有 `-RunAsAdmin`。和你贴的输出一字不差。

你运行的时机大概正好落在 push 修复后 raw CDN 那 ~5 分钟 TTL 的窗口里,拿到了旧副本。

## 怎么办

修复现在已经生效了(我这边抓到的就是新版,而且修复后又有 3 个提交,缓存早过期了)。**直接重跑同一条命令即可**:

```powershell
irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/daviddwlee84/dotfiles-windows/main/bootstrap.ps1 | iex
```

**怎么确认这次跑的是新版**:elevated 会话下,新脚本会打印这一行——

```
==> Installing scoop (elevated console: passing -RunAsAdmin)
```

看到括号里的 `passing -RunAsAdmin` 就说明走对分支了。如果 elevated 下还是只打印裸的 `==> Installing scoop`,那就是又拿到旧缓存了。

**万一还是旧缓存**,用 commit SHA 钉死版本(独立的 cache key,保证是修复版):

```powershell
irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/daviddwlee84/dotfiles-windows/52e7879/bootstrap.ps1 | iex
```

或者加个随机 query 破缓存:

```powershell
irm "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/daviddwlee84/dotfiles-windows/main/bootstrap.ps1?x=$(Get-Random)" | iex
```

---

代码本身不需要改。要不要我把「push 完 bootstrap 修复后、raw CDN 短时间内仍返回旧脚本 → 表现为『改了还是没用』」这个坑记进 `pitfalls/`?按 CLAUDE.md 的约定,这类非显而易见、可 grep 的排错正合适,以后你或其他 agent 再遇到 elevated 下 scoop abort 时能一眼定位是缓存而不是 `Test-Admin`。

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-10 15:54:25Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_30_windows_terminal.ps1`

```text
1	#Requires -Version 7
2	# run_onchange_after_30_windows_terminal.ps1 — set Windows Terminal's shared
3	# profile defaults (font, color, opacity) without touching individual profiles.
4	# No-op if WT isn't installed / hasn't created its settings.json yet.
5	
6	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'   # best-effort; never abort the apply
7	
8	$candidates = @()
9	$candidates += Get-ChildItem "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Packages\Microsoft.WindowsTerminal*\LocalState\settings.json" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object FullName
10	$candidates += "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Windows Terminal\settings.json"
11	$path = $candidates | Where-Object { Test-Path $_ } | Select-Object -First 1
12	
13	if (-not $path) { Write-Host 'Windows Terminal settings.json not found — launch WT once, then re-apply.'; return }
14	
15	$raw = Get-Content -Raw $path
16	
17	# Back up the pre-merge settings.json ONCE before we rewrite it. This merge is
18	# non-destructive (only profiles.defaults + defaultProfile change; your profile
19	# list / schemes / keybindings are preserved), but ConvertTo-Json reformats the
20	# file and strips comments, so keep an escape hatch. The run_once_before backup
21	# doesn't cover this package-versioned %LOCALAPPDATA% path.
22	$bak = Join-Path (Join-Path $HOME '.dotfiles-backup') 'windows-terminal-settings.json.bak'
23	if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $bak)) {
24	    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path (Split-Path $bak -Parent) | Out-Null
25	    Copy-Item -LiteralPath $path -Destination $bak -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
26	    Write-Host "backed up Windows Terminal settings -> $bak"
27	}
28	
29	$raw = [regex]::Replace($raw, '(?m)^\s*//.*$', '')   # drop whole-line comments
30	try { $s = $raw | ConvertFrom-Json -AsHashtable } catch { Write-Warning "could not parse $path — skipping"; return }
31	
32	if (-not $s.ContainsKey('profiles')) { $s['profiles'] = @{} }
33	if ($s.profiles -isnot [hashtable]) { $s['profiles'] = @{ list = $s.profiles } }
34	if (-not $s.profiles.ContainsKey('defaults')) { $s.profiles['defaults'] = @{} }
35	
36	$s.profiles.defaults['font'] = @{ face = 'Hack Nerd Font Mono'; size = 11 }
37	$s.profiles.defaults['colorScheme'] = 'One Half Dark'
38	$s.profiles.defaults['opacity'] = 90
39	$s.profiles.defaults['useAcrylic'] = $true
40	$s.profiles.defaults['cursorShape'] = 'filledBox'
41	$s.profiles.defaults['antialiasingMode'] = 'grayscale'
42	$s.profiles.defaults['padding'] = '8'
43	
44	# Prefer PowerShell 7 as the default profile when a matching profile exists.
45	if ($s.profiles.ContainsKey('list')) {
46	    $pwshProfile = @($s.profiles.list) |
47	        Where-Object { ($_.commandline -match 'pwsh') -or ($_.name -eq 'PowerShell') } |
48	        Select-Object -First 1
49	    if ($pwshProfile -and $pwshProfile.guid) { $s['defaultProfile'] = $pwshProfile.guid }
50	}
51	
52	$s | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 20 | Set-Content -Path $path -Encoding utf8
53	Write-Host "Windows Terminal defaults updated -> $path"
54
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-10 15:54:51Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
So the settings merge is non-destructive—it only modifies the defaults section and updates the default profile GUID if a matching PowerShell 7 profile exists, while keeping your existing profiles, color schemes, keybindings, and actions intact.

However, there are two important caveats: the script reformats the JSON and strips out comments (including whole-line `//` comments), so while your actual settings values survive, the file's formatting and comments don't. It does create a backup to `~/.dotfiles-backup/windows-terminal-settings.json.bak` before the first rewrite as a safety net, and it gracefully skips if the JSON can't be parsed or if Windows Terminal isn't installed.

For the second question, there are no `modify_` scripts in the repo at all—this is a `run_onchange_after` script, which aligns with the design principle that `modify_` interpreter selection is unreliable on Windows. The editors use the same run_onchange-merger approach.

Since it's a plain `.ps1` file (not templated), the "onchange" hash is just the file bytes themselves, so it re-runs whenever you edit the script, and each run reapplies the same defaults idempotently. The one real destructive aspect is that hand-added comments in the settings file get lost on each merge, though the backup mitigates that risk—the actual data like profiles, schemes, and keybindings stays intact.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-10 15:54:54Z)**_

两个问题的答案都确认了,直接看 `run_onchange_after_30_windows_terminal.ps1`:

## 会不会覆盖原本的 settings?

**不会覆盖你的内容 —— 是读入→合并→写回的非破坏性 merge**,不是整文件替换。脚本只改这几个 key:

- `profiles.defaults`(字体 `Hack Nerd Font Mono` / `colorScheme` / `opacity` / `useAcrylic` / `cursorShape` / `antialiasingMode` / `padding`)—— `.ps1:36-42`
- `defaultProfile`:仅当 profile list 里已存在 pwsh/PowerShell 的 profile 时,把默认改成它的 guid —— `.ps1:44-50`

你的 **profile 列表、配色方案(schemes)、键位(keybindings/actions)全部原样保留**(整个对象读进来又写回去)。

不过有**两点要如实说清楚**,严格讲不算"零改动":

1. **注释和格式会丢**。写回用的是 `ConvertTo-Json`,而且第 29 行会先把整行 `//` 注释删掉(`ConvertFrom-Json` 不接受注释)。所以你 settings.json 里手写的注释、缩进风格在合并后不复存在——数据在,排版和注释没了。
2. 这是**幂等**的:`run_onchange` 只在脚本内容变化时重跑,每次重跑都把上面那组 defaults 再压一遍(相同值)。

**安全网**:首次改写前会把原文件**备份一次**到 `~/.dotfiles-backup/windows-terminal-settings.json.bak`(`.ps1:22-27`)。另外解析失败会直接跳过不写(`.ps1:30`),WT 没装 / 还没生成 settings.json 也是 no-op(`.ps1:13`)。

## 有用 modify_ 吗?

**没有。** 全仓库 `find . -name 'modify_*'` 零结果。Windows Terminal 走的是 `run_onchange_after` 合并脚本,editor overlays(`run_onchange_after_20_editor_overlays.ps1.tmpl`)也是同一套路——这正是 CLAUDE.md 硬约束 5 说的:**在 Windows 上 `modify_` 的解释器选择不可靠,所以一律用 run_onchange 的 pwsh merger 代替**。

一句话:你现有的 profiles/schemes/keybindings 不会丢,但注释和格式会被 JSON 重新序列化;有一份一次性备份兜底。

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-10 16:02:23Z)**_

Read the managed loader — it's fully **location-independent**: it only computes `$HOME/.config/powershell/profile.d` and sources fragments from there (`Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1:7-27`). It has zero dependency on *where* it physically sits. That's the key to both the diagnosis and the fix.

## 什么在发生 —— 是的,这是我们的一个真 bug

链路是这样的:

1. chezmoi 把 managed loader 写到 **字面路径** `C:\Users\hanruzhou\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1`(它的 dest 是 `$HOME`,`Documents/...` 是字面 join,chezmoi 不查 OneDrive 已知文件夹)。
2. OneDrive KFM 把你的真实 Documents 挪到了 `OneDrive - Microsoft\Documents`,所以交互式 pwsh 的 `$PROFILE` 落在 OneDrive 那侧。
3. `run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1` 检测到两者不一致,就在 `$PROFILE`(OneDrive 侧)写了个 **stub**,第 4 行 `. "C:\Users\hanruzhou\Documents\PowerShell\..."` 去 source 字面路径 —— 你看到的报错正是这个 stub 的第 4 行。
4. **但那个字面路径的文件在这台机器上不存在**,于是 "not recognized as a name of a cmdlet..."。

**设计缺陷**:stub 假设「字面 `~/Documents` 那份 loader 一定存在」,而在 KFM 机器上这个假设不成立(字面 Documents 可能是被 OneDrive 搬空的残留空目录 / 反而是个 junction / chezmoi 那次没落到那里)。stub 用了个「指针」而不是「真身」,指针指向的目标却没了。

## 先让这台机器的 shell 现在就能用

managed loader 是自包含的,所以直接把它的**完整内容**灌进 OneDrive 侧的 `$PROFILE` 即可,不依赖那个缺失的字面文件:

```powershell
# 在 Windows 机器上跑:
chezmoi cat "$HOME\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1" |
    Set-Content -LiteralPath $PROFILE -Encoding utf8
. $PROFILE          # 立刻加载,应能看到 starship / aliases 回来
```

`chezmoi cat` 直接从 source 渲染内容,不需要磁盘上那份存在。这一步同时也**验证了修复思路**。

## 顺手采一个诊断(帮我确认精确拓扑,好写对 CI)

麻烦把这几行的输出贴给我,能定死到底是「字面 Documents 是 junction」还是「被搬空的残留空目录」:

```powershell
$lit = "$HOME\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1"
[pscustomobject]@{
  Profile      = $PROFILE
  LiteralExists= Test-Path $lit
  DocsAttrs    = (Get-Item "$HOME\Documents" -Force).Attributes   # 看有没有 ReparsePoint
  ManagedByCM  = (chezmoi source-path $lit 2>$null)
} | Format-List
```

## 永久修复方案

把 `run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1` 从「写指针 stub」改成「写真身」:检测到重定向时,**把 managed loader 的完整内容**写进 `$real`,而不是 `. "<字面路径>"`。因为 loader 本身只认 `~/.config/powershell`,放哪都能跑,这样就彻底摆脱对字面 `~/Documents` 副本存在与否的依赖。

要做对,需要连带处理几个 repo 约束:

- 脚本要拿到 loader 内容 → 改成 `.tmpl`,用 `{{ include "Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1" }}` 内联。**好处**:loader 内容进了 rendered 脚本,`run_onchange` 会在 loader 变化时正确重跑(现在的纯 `.ps1` 版本不会)。
- 保留现有的一次性备份逻辑(`~/.dotfiles-backup/...onedrive.bak`)。
- 按硬约束 3:`.ps1.tmpl` 要在 `windows.yml` 里 render+parse,别引入解析错误。
- 值得配一个 `pitfalls/onedrive-kfm-profile-not-loaded.md`(verbatim 那条 "not recognized as a name of a cmdlet" 报错,方便以后 grep)。

这是个跨 run-script + 模板化 + CI 的非平凡改动。要我**进 plan mode** 把完整实现方案列出来给你过一遍吗?或者你先把上面诊断的输出贴我,我据此把 fix 一次写到位。

---

The `uv python install --default` command is failing because the current version of uv (0.11.28) requires the `--preview` flag to use `--default` — that's a straightforward fix. There's also a `winget` install failure for a Microsoft Store package that's worth investigating further.

The real bottleneck though is that profile load time. I need to dig into which part of the PowerShell initialization is causing the slowdown — could be starship, zoxide, the Copilot module, PSReadLine, or various tool detection commands.  Scoop shims and clipboard operations are also suspects. Without running this on Windows directly, I can't pinpoint exactly which fragment is expensive, but I should inspect the profile.d files for obvious culprits like network calls or subprocess spawns.

The GitHub authentication warning is expected and not a real issue. There's also something about btop's pre_install that got cut off, but I should look into that too. Adding `vi`/`vim` aliases to point to `nvim` would be a quick UX win. The real issue though is the redirect stub timing — on the first `chezmoi init --apply` during bootstrap, the profile wasn't loaded yet, so the stub didn't get written until they manually ran `chezmoi apply` again.

The "file not found" error they saw earlier was because the stub was pointing at a literal path that didn't exist yet. Once the full apply completed and the managed loader file got created, the stub worked fine — opening a new tab no longer throws warnings. So it was a transient ordering problem that resolved itself.

But now I'm noticing something odd about the bootstrap process: the initial `chezmoi init --apply` only prompted for three things (role, name, email) and then finished without asking for all the install toggles. That seems wrong — it should have presented the full set of prompts. So the sequence makes sense now: the first init only prompted for role/name/email because `promptBool` fields for the install toggles must have had defaults that were used, or stdin wasn't a TTY so chezmoi skipped those prompts and applied defaults. Then when the user ran `chezmoi init` without `--apply`, it prompted for everything and populated the config with all the toggle values. After that, `chezmoi apply` and subsequent updates could actually run the package installations since the config was now complete.

The real problem is that piping the bootstrap script through `irm | iex` breaks the interactive prompts — stdin gets consumed by the script itself, so chezmoi can't properly collect all the user's preferences, forcing them to manually run `chezmoi init` afterward to set everything up correctly. The core issue is that piping stdin into the bootstrap script causes `chezmoi init` to hit EOF after the first few prompts, so the remaining boolean toggles default to false and skip most package installations. This is a fundamental limitation of running interactive prompts through a pipe. The better approach would be for bootstrap to skip the `chezmoi init --apply` step entirely and instead guide the user to run it manually in an interactive terminal where all prompts can be answered properly.

But stepping back, the user is asking me to focus on the specific OneDrive redirect fix they mentioned, and to identify any other improvements worth bundling in. Starship and zoxide both spawn subprocesses that take 100-300ms each. Importing Copilot could be slow if it does network calls on load. The tv cache might regenerate at startup. OneDrive synced folders are a suspect too—if the profile files are cloud-only, reading them could cause delays. The managed loader dot-sourcing from the config directory is likely the biggest culprit here.

On a corporate Microsoft machine, antivirus scanning every subprocess spawn adds up fast—starship shells out to git for the prompt, zoxide, tv, and other tools all get scanned. The Copilot module import might be starting the copilot-api proxy or checking for bun, which could be expensive.

The 8.8-second startup time points to either a network call happening at load, many subprocesses getting hammered by antivirus, or the Copilot proxy doing something heavy. I need to dig into the fragments to find which operation is actually expensive.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./dot_config/powershell/profile.d/00_env.ps1`

```text
1	# 00_env.ps1 — environment variables & PATH. Sourced first by $PROFILE.
2	
3	# UTF-8 console I/O. Without this, native tools that emit UTF-8 (git, winget's
4	# localized output, …) render as mojibake on legacy-codepage systems (e.g. GBK /
5	# cp936 on zh-* Windows). Guarded so a redirected/headless host can't error the
6	# rest of this fragment.
7	try {
8	    $utf8 = [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false)
9	    [Console]::OutputEncoding = $utf8
10	    $OutputEncoding = $utf8
11	} catch { $null = $_ }
12	
13	# XDG-style base dirs. Several tools honor these on Windows (starship, atuin,
14	# zoxide state, our own copilot module) so we set them for a tidy $HOME.
15	if (-not $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME) { $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME = Join-Path $HOME '.config' }
16	if (-not $env:XDG_DATA_HOME)   { $env:XDG_DATA_HOME   = Join-Path $HOME '.local/share' }
17	if (-not $env:XDG_STATE_HOME)  { $env:XDG_STATE_HOME  = Join-Path $HOME '.local/state' }
18	if (-not $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME)  { $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME  = Join-Path $HOME '.cache' }
19	
20	$env:STARSHIP_CONFIG = Join-Path $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME 'starship.toml'
21	# yazi looks in %APPDATA%\yazi\config by default; keep it XDG-consistent instead.
22	$env:YAZI_CONFIG_HOME = Join-Path $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME 'yazi'
23	
24	# uv-managed Python: `uv python install --default` drops python/python3 here.
25	# ~/.local/bin is prepended to PATH below, so they win over the Windows Store
26	# `python.exe` app-execution-alias.
27	$env:UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR = Join-Path $HOME '.local/bin'
28	
29	# Default editor
30	if (Get-Command nvim -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) { $env:EDITOR = 'nvim' }
31	
32	# Prepend user bin dirs to PATH, idempotently. scoop already adds its shims to
33	# the persisted user PATH, but a fresh session inside chezmoi apply may not have
34	# it yet; ~/.local/bin carries uv tools + the uv-managed python. Normalize each
35	# entry to backslashes (mixed slashes can fail Windows command resolution).
36	$UserPaths = @(
37	    (Join-Path $HOME '.local/bin'),
38	    (Join-Path $HOME 'scoop/shims')
39	) | Where-Object { $_ -and (Test-Path $_) } |
40	    ForEach-Object { [System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath($_) } | Select-Object -Unique
41	
42	$SepPaths = $env:PATH -split ';'
43	foreach ($p in $UserPaths) {
44	    if ($SepPaths -notcontains $p) { $env:PATH = "$p;$env:PATH" }
45	}
46
```

```text
21	
22	# fzf — fuzzy finder + PSFzf key bindings (Ctrl+t files, Ctrl+r history)
23	if (Get-Command fzf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
24	    $env:FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS = '--height 40% --layout=reverse --border'
25	    if (Get-Command fd -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
26	        $env:FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND = 'fd --hidden --strip-cwd-prefix --exclude .git'
27	    }
28	    if (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name PSFzf) {
29	        Import-Module PSFzf
30	        Set-PsFzfOption -PSReadlineChordProvider 'Ctrl+t' -PSReadlineChordReverseHistory 'Ctrl+r'
31	    }
32	}
33	
34	# direnv — per-directory environments
35	if (Get-Command direnv -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
36	    Invoke-Expression (& direnv hook pwsh | Out-String)
37	}
38	
39	# television (tv) — fuzzy picker shell integration. tv 0.15+ renamed the shell
40	# value `powershell` -> `power-shell`; older builds used `powershell`. Try the
41	# new spelling first, fall back, and only eval non-empty output so a version
42	# mismatch never errors the prompt.
43	if (Get-Command tv -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
44	    $tvInit = (tv init power-shell 2>$null | Out-String)
45	    if (-not $tvInit.Trim()) { $tvInit = (tv init powershell 2>$null | Out-String) }
46	    if ($tvInit.Trim()) { try { Invoke-Expression $tvInit } catch { $null = $_ } }
47	}
```

```text
1	# 20_aliases.ps1 — aliases and small helper functions. Modern-CLI replacements
2	# are guarded so they only shadow the builtin when the tool is installed.
3	
4	# --- modern replacements ---
5	if (Get-Command eza -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
6	    function ll { eza -l  --icons --git @args }
7	    function la { eza -la --icons --git @args }
8	    function lt { eza --tree --level=2 --icons @args }
9	    Remove-Item Alias:ls -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
10	    function ls { eza --icons @args }
11	}
12	if (Get-Command bat -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
13	    Remove-Item Alias:cat -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
14	    function cat { bat @args }
15	    if (-not $env:BAT_THEME) { $env:BAT_THEME = 'ansi' }
16	}
17	if (Get-Command nvim -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
18	    Set-Alias -Name vim -Value nvim -Scope Global
19	    Set-Alias -Name vi  -Value nvim -Scope Global
20	}
21	if (Get-Command lazygit -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
22	    Set-Alias -Name lg -Value lazygit -Scope Global
23	}
24	
25	# --- unix muscle-memory ---
26	# `which foo` — resolve a command like the unix tool. pwsh's native equivalent
27	# is Get-Command (alias gcm); this prints just the path for executables and a
28	# short description for aliases / functions / cmdlets.
29	function which {
30	    param([Parameter(Mandatory, ValueFromRemainingArguments)][string[]]$Name)
31	    foreach ($n in $Name) {
32	        $c = Get-Command $n -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
33	        if (-not $c) { Write-Error "which: $n not found"; continue }
34	        switch ($c.CommandType) {
35	            'Application' { $c.Source }
36	            'Alias'       { '{0}: aliased to {1}' -f $n, $c.Definition }
37	            default       { '{0}: {1}' -f $n, $c.CommandType }
38	        }
39	    }
40	}
41	
42	# unix / mac command names → Windows equivalents (muscle memory)
43	function ifconfig { ipconfig @args }
44	function open { Invoke-Item @args }          # open a file/dir in its default app
45	function pbcopy { $input | Set-Clipboard }   # pipe into it: some-cmd | pbcopy
46	function pbpaste { Get-Clipboard }
47	function touch {
48	    param([Parameter(Mandatory, ValueFromRemainingArguments)][string[]]$Path)
49	    foreach ($p in $Path) {
50	        if (Test-Path -LiteralPath $p) { (Get-Item -LiteralPath $p).LastWriteTime = Get-Date }
51	        else { New-Item -ItemType File -Path $p -Force | Out-Null }
52	    }
53	}
54	
55	# --- git shortcuts ---
56	function gs { git status @args }
57	function gd { git diff @args }
58	function ga { git add @args }
59	function gc { git commit @args }
60	function gp { git push @args }
61	function gl { git log --oneline --graph --decorate @args }
62	
63	# --- shell / dotfiles management ---
64	# reload the current session's profile
65	function reload { . $PROFILE }
66	# jump to the chezmoi source dir
67	function chezmoi-cd { Set-Location (chezmoi source-path) }
68	# chezmoi apply / update, then reload (twins of the unix `cas` / `cau`)
69	function cas { chezmoi apply @args; . $PROFILE }
70	function cau { chezmoi update @args; . $PROFILE }
71	
72	# --- run-for: time-box an external command (e.g. `run-for 5 ping example.com`) ---
73	function run-for {
74	    param(
75	        [Parameter(Mandatory)][int]$Seconds,
76	        [Parameter(Mandatory, ValueFromRemainingArguments)][string[]]$Command
77	    )
78	    $rest = if ($Command.Count -gt 1) { $Command[1..($Command.Count - 1)] } else { @() }
79	    $p = Start-Process -FilePath $Command[0] -ArgumentList $rest -PassThru -NoNewWindow
80	    if (-not $p.WaitForExit($Seconds * 1000)) {
81	        $p.Kill()
82	        Write-Warning "run-for: timed out after ${Seconds}s"
83	    }
84	}
```

```text
1	# 28_tldr.ps1 — tldr language-preference helper.
2	#
3	# Native PowerShell port of the cross-platform dot_config/shell/28_tldr.sh.
4	# `tldrf` runs the tldr client (tlrc, installed via scoop) with a fallback chain
5	# of languages: tlrc accepts `-L` multiple times and tries them in order, so a
6	# page missing in zh_TW falls back to zh, then en. Plain `tldr` keeps its native
7	# behaviour. Override the order in your untracked local.ps1 if you like.
8	
9	# Language preference order for tldrf fallback.
10	$TLDR_LANGUAGES = @('zh_TW', 'zh', 'en')
11	
12	function tldrf {
13	    if (-not (Get-Command tldr -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { return }
14	    $langFlags = foreach ($lang in $TLDR_LANGUAGES) { '-L'; $lang }
15	    & tldr @langFlags @args
16	}
17
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# 30_apps.ps1 — app control, system audio, and clipboard helpers. Native
2	# PowerShell equivalents of the macOS/Linux app*/sys*/x helpers, same verb names.
3	
4	# --- app control (applaunch / appquit / apprestart / apprunning / applist) ---
5	function applaunch {
6	    param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Name)
7	    Start-Process $Name
8	}
9	function appquit {
10	    param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Name)
11	    $proc = $Name -replace '\.exe$', ''
12	    Get-Process -Name $proc -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Stop-Process -Force
13	}
14	function apprestart {
15	    param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Name)
16	    appquit $Name; Start-Sleep 1; applaunch $Name
17	}
18	function apprunning {
19	    param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Name)
20	    [bool](Get-Process -Name ($Name -replace '\.exe$', '') -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
21	}
22	function applist {
23	    Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.MainWindowTitle } | Select-Object Name, Id, MainWindowTitle | Sort-Object Name
24	}
25	
26	# --- system audio (sysvol / sysmute) ---
27	# Absolute level + explicit mute use AudioDeviceCmdlets when installed; a
28	# dependency-free media-key fallback covers up/down and mute-toggle everywhere.
29	function script:Initialize-VolumeKeys {
30	    if (-not ('Native.WinVol' -as [type])) {
31	        Add-Type -Name WinVol -Namespace Native -MemberDefinition @'
32	[System.Runtime.InteropServices.DllImport("user32.dll")]
33	public static extern void keybd_event(byte bVk, byte bScan, uint dwFlags, System.UIntPtr dwExtraInfo);
34	'@
35	    }
36	}
37	function script:Send-VolumeKey {
38	    param([byte]$Vk, [int]$Times = 1)
39	    Initialize-VolumeKeys
40	    for ($i = 0; $i -lt $Times; $i++) { [Native.WinVol]::keybd_event($Vk, 0, 0, [System.UIntPtr]::Zero) }
41	}
42	
43	function sysvol {
44	    param([Parameter(Position = 0)][string]$Arg, [Parameter(Position = 1)][int]$Amount = 10)
45	    $hasCmdlet = [bool](Get-Command Set-AudioDevice -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
46	    switch ($Arg) {
47	        'up'   { if ($hasCmdlet) { $c = [int]((Get-AudioDevice -PlaybackVolume) -replace '\D'); Set-AudioDevice -PlaybackVolume ([math]::Min(100, $c + $Amount)) } else { Send-VolumeKey 0xAF ([math]::Ceiling($Amount / 2)) } }
48	        'down' { if ($hasCmdlet) { $c = [int]((Get-AudioDevice -PlaybackVolume) -replace '\D'); Set-AudioDevice -PlaybackVolume ([math]::Max(0, $c - $Amount)) } else { Send-VolumeKey 0xAE ([math]::Ceiling($Amount / 2)) } }
49	        ''     { if ($hasCmdlet) { Get-AudioDevice -PlaybackVolume } else { Write-Warning 'sysvol: install AudioDeviceCmdlets for level readout; use `sysvol up|down`' } }
50	        default {
51	            if ($Arg -match '^\d+$') {
52	                if ($hasCmdlet) { Set-AudioDevice -PlaybackVolume ([int]$Arg) }
53	                else { Write-Warning 'sysvol <N> (absolute) needs AudioDeviceCmdlets; use `sysvol up|down` instead' }
54	            } else { Write-Warning "sysvol: usage: sysvol [up|down [step] | <0-100>]" }
55	        }
56	    }
57	}
58	
59	function sysmute {
60	    param([Parameter(Position = 0)][ValidateSet('', 'on', 'off', 'toggle')][string]$State = 'toggle')
61	    if (Get-Command Set-AudioDevice -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
62	        switch ($State) {
63	            'on'  { Set-AudioDevice -PlaybackMute $true }
64	            'off' { Set-AudioDevice -PlaybackMute $false }
65	            default { Set-AudioDevice -PlaybackMuteToggle }
66	        }
67	    } else {
68	        Send-VolumeKey 0xAD 1   # VK_VOLUME_MUTE toggles
69	        if ($State -in 'on', 'off') { Write-Warning 'sysmute on/off needs AudioDeviceCmdlets; toggled instead' }
70	    }
71	}
72	
73	# --- clipboard / open (x) ---
74	# x copy [text]   copy args (or piped stdin) to the clipboard
75	# x paste         print the clipboard
76	# x open <target> open a file/URL with its default handler
77	function x {
78	    param(
79	        [Parameter(Mandatory, Position = 0)][ValidateSet('copy', 'paste', 'open')][string]$Action,
80	        [Parameter(ValueFromRemainingArguments)][string[]]$Rest
81	    )
82	    switch ($Action) {
83	        'copy'  {
84	            $data = if ($Rest) { $Rest -join ' ' } else { ($input | Out-String).TrimEnd("`r", "`n") }
85	            Set-Clipboard -Value $data
86	        }
87	        'paste' { Get-Clipboard }
88	        'open'  { if ($Rest) { Start-Process ($Rest -join ' ') } else { Start-Process . } }
89	    }
90	}
91
```

```text
1	# 35_yazi.ps1 — `y` launches yazi and cd's to wherever you quit (mirrors the
2	# unix y() wrapper). No-op if yazi isn't installed.
3	if (Get-Command yazi -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
4	    function y {
5	        $tmp = New-TemporaryFile
6	        try {
7	            yazi @args --cwd-file $tmp.FullName
8	            $cwd = (Get-Content -Raw -LiteralPath $tmp.FullName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Trim()
9	            if ($cwd -and $cwd -ne $PWD.Path -and (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cwd)) {
10	                Set-Location -LiteralPath $cwd
11	            }
12	        } finally {
13	            Remove-Item -LiteralPath $tmp.FullName -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
14	        }
15	    }
16	}
```

```text
1	# 98_tv_cache.ps1 — precompute the alias/function list for the `tv aliases`
2	# channel. tv runs channel commands in a fresh shell without our $PROFILE, so we
3	# snapshot the current session's aliases + functions here (runs late, after the
4	# other fragments have defined theirs). No-op if tv isn't installed.
5	if (Get-Command tv -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
6	    $dir = Join-Path $HOME '.cache/tv'
7	    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $dir | Out-Null
8	    $lines = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]::new()
9	    Get-Alias | Sort-Object Name | ForEach-Object { $lines.Add("$($_.Name) -> $($_.Definition)") }
10	    Get-Command -CommandType Function -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
11	        Where-Object { -not $_.Source -or $_.Source -eq 'Copilot' } |
12	        Sort-Object Name | ForEach-Object { $lines.Add("$($_.Name) (function)") }
13	    $lines | Set-Content -Path (Join-Path $dir 'aliases.txt') -Encoding utf8
14	}
15
```

```text
1	# Copilot.psm1 — GitHub Copilot -> Anthropic proxy for Claude Code, native
2	# PowerShell port of the POSIX 43_copilot_proxy.sh / 44_copilot_embed.sh.
3	#
4	# Runs the maintained copilot-api fork (npm @jeffreycao/copilot-api) via `bunx`
5	# so a GitHub Copilot subscription can back Claude Code. The optional Bun
6	# throttle shim (copilot-throttle-shim.js) is reused verbatim from the unix side.
7	#
8	# Public commands (exported with their original hyphenated names for muscle
9	# memory — PSUseApprovedVerbs is intentionally waived):
10	#   copilot-proxy [start|stop|restart|status|doctor [--live]|logs [shim|N]|shim [on|off|status]|whoami|auth]
11	#   copilot-run <cmd...>            run a command with the proxy env injected
12	#   claude-copilot [args...]        one-off Claude Code session on the proxy
13	#   claude-copilot-once [args...]   pinned one-shot session, auto-reverted
14	#   copilot-here [on|off|status]    per-project pin via ./.claude/settings.local.json
15	#   copilot-model [<id>|-l|-c]      switch the pinned model
16	#   copilot-embed [--model M] [--json] [TEXT|-] | -l
17	#   semsearch index [PATH...] | semsearch <QUERY> [-k N] [--corpus PATH]
18	#
19	# Contract preserved from the unix version: token at
20	# ~/.local/share/copilot-api/github_token, ports 4141 (proxy) / 4142 (shim),
21	# state under $XDG_STATE_HOME/copilot-proxy, per-project ./.claude/settings.local.json
22	# pin, default model claude-opus-4-8[1m], and the ANTHROPIC_* env block.
23	
24	Set-StrictMode -Off
25	
26	# ------------------------------------------------------------------ helpers ---
27	function script:Get-CopilotPort { if ($env:COPILOT_PROXY_PORT) { $env:COPILOT_PROXY_PORT } else { '4141' } }
28	function script:Get-CopilotPkg  { if ($env:COPILOT_API_PKG)   { $env:COPILOT_API_PKG }   else { '@jeffreycao/copilot-api@1.13.14' } }
29	function script:Get-CopilotPkgFlavor {
30	    switch -Regex (Get-CopilotPkg) { '^copilot-api(@.*)?$' { 'original' } default { 'fork' } }
31	}
32	function script:Get-CopilotBase    { "http://localhost:$(Get-CopilotPort)" }
33	function script:Get-CopilotTmp     { if ($env:TEMP) { $env:TEMP } else { [System.IO.Path]::GetTempPath() } }
34	function script:Get-CopilotLogFile { Join-Path (Get-CopilotTmp) "copilot-api-$(Get-CopilotPort).log" }
35	function script:Get-CopilotPidFile { Join-Path (Get-CopilotTmp) "copilot-api-$(Get-CopilotPort).pid" }
36	function script:Get-CopilotToken   { Join-Path $HOME '.local/share/copilot-api/github_token' }
37	
38	function script:Get-XdgState { if ($env:XDG_STATE_HOME) { $env:XDG_STATE_HOME } else { Join-Path $HOME '.local/state' } }
39	function script:Get-XdgConfig { if ($env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME) { $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME } else { Join-Path $HOME '.config' } }
40	
41	# --- shim paths ---
42	function script:Get-CopilotShimPort   { if ($env:COPILOT_SHIM_PORT) { $env:COPILOT_SHIM_PORT } else { '4142' } }
43	function script:Get-CopilotShimBase   { "http://localhost:$(Get-CopilotShimPort)" }
44	function script:Get-CopilotShimScript { Join-Path (Get-XdgConfig) 'powershell/copilot-throttle-shim.js' }
45	function script:Get-CopilotShimLog    { Join-Path (Get-CopilotTmp) "copilot-shim-$(Get-CopilotShimPort).log" }
46	function script:Get-CopilotShimPid    { Join-Path (Get-CopilotTmp) "copilot-shim-$(Get-CopilotShimPort).pid" }
47	function script:Get-CopilotShimState  { Join-Path (Get-XdgState) 'copilot-proxy/shim' }
48	function script:Get-CopilotModelState { Join-Path (Get-XdgState) 'copilot-proxy/model' }
49	
50	# The bun launcher: prefer `bunx`, else `bun x`.
51	function script:Get-BunLauncher {
52	    if (Get-Command bunx -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) { return @{ Exe = 'bunx'; Pre = @() } }
53	    if (Get-Command bun  -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) { return @{ Exe = 'bun';  Pre = @('x') } }
54	    return $null
55	}
56	
57	function script:Test-CopilotAlive {
58	    try { $null = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$(Get-CopilotBase)/v1/models" -TimeoutSec 2 -ErrorAction Stop; $true }
59	    catch { $false }
60	}
61	function script:Test-CopilotShimAlive {
62	    try { $null = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "$(Get-CopilotShimBase)/v1/models" -TimeoutSec 2 -SkipHttpErrorCheck -ErrorAction Stop; $true }
63	    catch { $false }
64	}
65	function script:Get-CopilotShimEnabled {
66	    switch ($env:COPILOT_PROXY_SHIM) {
67	        { $_ -in '1', 'on', 'true', 'yes' } { return $true }
68	        { $_ -in '0', 'off', 'false', 'no' } { return $false }
69	    }
70	    $sf = Get-CopilotShimState
71	    (Test-Path $sf) -and ((Get-Content -First 1 $sf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) -eq 'on')
72	}
73	function script:Get-CopilotClientBase {
74	    if ((Get-CopilotShimEnabled) -and (Test-CopilotShimAlive)) { Get-CopilotShimBase } else { Get-CopilotBase }
75	}
76	function script:Get-CopilotPinnedBase {
77	    if (Get-CopilotShimEnabled) { Get-CopilotShimBase } else { Get-CopilotBase }
78	}
79	
80	# Resolve the model: $COPILOT_CLAUDE_MODEL > state file > default.
81	function script:Get-CopilotDefaultModel {
82	    if ($env:COPILOT_CLAUDE_MODEL) { return $env:COPILOT_CLAUDE_MODEL }
83	    $sf = Get-CopilotModelState
84	    if (Test-Path $sf) { return (Get-Content -First 1 $sf) }
85	    'claude-opus-4-8[1m]'
86	}
87	
88	# Every model id the proxy accepts: .id plus the .claude_model_id alias.
89	function script:Get-CopilotServedModels {
90	    try { $r = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$(Get-CopilotBase)/v1/models" -TimeoutSec 5 -ErrorAction Stop } catch { return @() }
91	    $ids = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]::new()
92	    foreach ($m in $r.data) {
93	        if ($m.id) { $ids.Add($m.id) }
94	        if ($m.claude_model_id) { $ids.Add($m.claude_model_id) }
95	    }
96	    $ids | Sort-Object -Unique
97	}
98	
99	# "<model>|<source>" — the model Claude Code would send from this directory.
100	function script:Get-CopilotEffectiveModel {
101	    $settings = '.claude/settings.local.json'
102	    if (Test-Path $settings) {
103	        try {
104	            $j = Get-Content -Raw $settings | ConvertFrom-Json
105	            if ($j.env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL) {
106	                $m = $j.env.ANTHROPIC_MODEL
107	                if ($m) { return "$m|project pin: $settings" }
108	            }
109	        } catch { $null = $_ }
110	    }
111	    if ($env:COPILOT_CLAUDE_MODEL) { return "$($env:COPILOT_CLAUDE_MODEL)|`$COPILOT_CLAUDE_MODEL" }
112	    $sf = Get-CopilotModelState
113	    if (Test-Path $sf) { return "$(Get-Content -First 1 $sf)|state file: $sf" }
114	    "$(Get-CopilotDefaultModel)|built-in default"
115	}
116	
117	# --- shim start/stop ---
118	function script:Start-CopilotShim {
119	    if (Test-CopilotShimAlive) { return $true }
120	    if (-not (Get-Command bun -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) {
121	        Write-Error "copilot-proxy: shim needs 'bun' (scoop install bun)"; return $false
122	    }
123	    $script = Get-CopilotShimScript
124	    if (-not (Test-Path $script)) { Write-Error "copilot-proxy: shim script not found at $script"; return $false }
125	    $env:COPILOT_SHIM_PORT = Get-CopilotShimPort
126	    $env:COPILOT_SHIM_UPSTREAM = Get-CopilotBase
127	    $p = Start-Process -FilePath 'bun' -ArgumentList @($script) -PassThru -WindowStyle Hidden `
128	        -RedirectStandardOutput (Get-CopilotShimLog) -RedirectStandardError "$(Get-CopilotShimLog).err"
129	    $p.Id | Set-Content -Path (Get-CopilotShimPid)
130	    for ($i = 0; $i -lt 10; $i++) { if (Test-CopilotShimAlive) { return $true }; Start-Sleep 1 }
131	    Write-Error "copilot-proxy: shim did not come up — check $(Get-CopilotShimLog)"; $false
132	}
133	function script:Stop-CopilotShim {
134	    $pidf = Get-CopilotShimPid
135	    if (Test-Path $pidf) {
136	        $pid_ = Get-Content -First 1 $pidf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
137	        if ($pid_) { Stop-Process -Id $pid_ -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }
138	        Remove-Item $pidf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
139	    }
140	    Get-CimInstance Win32_Process -Filter "Name = 'bun.exe'" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
141	        Where-Object { $_.CommandLine -like '*copilot-throttle-shim.js*' } |
142	        ForEach-Object { Stop-Process -Id $_.ProcessId -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }
143	}
144	
145	# ============================================================ copilot-proxy ===
146	function copilot-proxy {
147	    [CmdletBinding()]
148	    param([Parameter(ValueFromRemainingArguments)] [string[]] $Argv)
149	
150	    $bun = Get-BunLauncher
151	    if (-not $bun) { Write-Error "copilot-proxy: bun/bunx not found (scoop install bun)"; return }
152	
153	    $port = Get-CopilotPort; $pkg = Get-CopilotPkg
154	    $logf = Get-CopilotLogFile; $pidf = Get-CopilotPidFile
155	    $action = if ($Argv -and $Argv.Count -ge 1) { $Argv[0] } else { 'status' }
156	
157	    switch ($action) {
158	        'start' {
159	            if (Test-CopilotAlive) { Write-Host "copilot-proxy: already running on port $port"; return }
160	            if (-not (Test-Path (Get-CopilotToken))) {
161	                Write-Error "copilot-proxy: not authenticated yet — run 'copilot-proxy auth' first."; return
162	            }
163	            # Rotate the previous session's log (keep last 3).
164	            if (Test-Path $logf) {
165	                Remove-Item "$logf.3" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
166	                if (Test-Path "$logf.2") { Move-Item -Force "$logf.2" "$logf.3" }
167	                if (Test-Path "$logf.1") { Move-Item -Force "$logf.1" "$logf.2" }
168	                Move-Item -Force $logf "$logf.1"
169	            }
170	            $argList = @($bun.Pre + @($pkg, 'start', '--port', $port))
171	            if ((Get-CopilotPkgFlavor) -eq 'original') {
172	                $rate = if ($env:COPILOT_PROXY_RATE) { $env:COPILOT_PROXY_RATE } else { '15' }
173	                $argList += @('--rate-limit', $rate, '--wait')
174	                Write-Host "copilot-proxy: starting ($pkg) on port $port (rate-limit ${rate}s) ..."
175	            } else {
176	                Write-Host "copilot-proxy: starting ($pkg) on port $port ..."
177	            }
178	            $p = Start-Process -FilePath $bun.Exe -ArgumentList $argList -PassThru -WindowStyle Hidden `
179	                -RedirectStandardOutput $logf -RedirectStandardError "$logf.err"
180	            $p.Id | Set-Content -Path $pidf
181	            for ($i = 0; $i -lt 20; $i++) {
182	                if (Test-CopilotAlive) {
183	                    if (Get-CopilotShimEnabled) {
184	                        if (Start-CopilotShim) { Write-Host "copilot-proxy: throttle shim up -> $(Get-CopilotShimBase) (-> $(Get-CopilotBase))" }
185	                    }
186	                    Write-Host "copilot-proxy: up -> $(Get-CopilotClientBase)  (logs: copilot-proxy logs)"
187	                    return
188	                }
189	                Start-Sleep 1
190	            }
191	            Write-Error "copilot-proxy: did not come up in time — check 'copilot-proxy logs'."
192	        }
193	        'stop' {
194	            Stop-CopilotShim
195	            if (Test-Path $pidf) {
196	                $pid_ = Get-Content -First 1 $pidf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
197	                if ($pid_) { Stop-Process -Id $pid_ -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }
198	                Remove-Item $pidf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
199	            }
200	            Get-CimInstance Win32_Process -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
201	                Where-Object { $_.CommandLine -like "*copilot-api*--port $port*" } |
202	                ForEach-Object { Stop-Process -Id $_.ProcessId -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }
203	            Start-Sleep 1
204	            if (Test-CopilotAlive) { Write-Error "copilot-proxy: still answering on $port (another instance?)"; return }
205	            Write-Host "copilot-proxy: stopped (port $port free)"
206	        }
207	        'restart' { copilot-proxy stop; copilot-proxy start }
208	        'status' {
209	            if (Test-CopilotAlive) {
210	                Write-Host "copilot-proxy: RUNNING on $(Get-CopilotBase)"
211	                $claude = (Get-CopilotServedModels) | Where-Object { $_ -match 'claude' }
212	                Write-Host "  models: $($claude -join ' ')"
213	                if (Get-CopilotShimEnabled) {
214	                    if (Test-CopilotShimAlive) { Write-Host "  shim:   ON, up on $(Get-CopilotShimBase)  -> clients use this" }
215	                    else { Write-Host "  shim:   ON but DOWN (clients fall back to $(Get-CopilotBase))" }
216	                } else { Write-Host "  shim:   off  (enable: copilot-proxy shim on)" }
217	            } else {
218	                Write-Host "copilot-proxy: not running on port $port  (start: copilot-proxy start)"
219	            }
220	        }
221	        { $_ -in 'doctor', 'test' } { Invoke-CopilotDoctor -Live:($Argv -contains '--live') }
222	        'logs' {
223	            if ($Argv.Count -ge 2 -and $Argv[1] -eq 'shim') {
224	                $lf = Get-CopilotShimLog; $n = if ($Argv.Count -ge 3) { [int]$Argv[2] } else { 40 }
225	            } else {
226	                $lf = $logf; $n = if ($Argv.Count -ge 2) { [int]$Argv[1] } else { 40 }
227	                if ($Argv.Count -ge 3 -and $Argv[2] -in '1', '2', '3') { $lf = "$logf.$($Argv[2])" }
228	            }
229	            if (Test-Path $lf) { Get-Content -Tail $n $lf }
230	            elseif (Test-Path "$lf.err") { Get-Content -Tail $n "$lf.err" }
231	            else { Write-Error "copilot-proxy: no log file at $lf" }
232	        }
233	        'auth' {
234	            Write-Host "copilot-proxy: launching copilot-api device login ..."
235	            & $bun.Exe @($bun.Pre + @($pkg, 'auth'))
236	        }
237	        { $_ -in 'whoami', 'usage' } {
238	            if (-not (Test-Path (Get-CopilotToken))) { Write-Error "copilot-proxy: not authenticated — run 'copilot-proxy auth'."; return }
239	            if ((Get-CopilotPkgFlavor) -eq 'original') { & $bun.Exe @($bun.Pre + @($pkg, 'check-usage')) }
240	            elseif (Test-CopilotAlive) {
241	                try {
242	                    $u = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$(Get-CopilotBase)/usage" -TimeoutSec 5 -ErrorAction Stop
243	                    [pscustomobject]@{
244	                        plan        = if ($u.copilot_plan) { $u.copilot_plan } elseif ($u.access_type_sku) { $u.access_type_sku } else { 'unknown' }
245	                        quota_reset = $u.quota_reset_date
246	                        quotas      = $u.quota_snapshots
247	                    } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 6
248	                } catch { Write-Error "copilot-proxy: /usage query failed: $_" }
249	            } else {
250	                Write-Host "copilot-proxy: not running — showing auth/debug info instead of quota."
251	                & $bun.Exe @($bun.Pre + @($pkg, 'debug'))
252	            }
253	        }
254	        'shim' {
255	            $sf = Get-CopilotShimState
256	            $sub = if ($Argv.Count -ge 2) { $Argv[1] } else { 'status' }
257	            switch ($sub) {
258	                'on' {
259	                    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path (Split-Path $sf) | Out-Null
260	                    'on' | Set-Content $sf
261	                    if (Test-CopilotAlive) { if (Start-CopilotShim) { Write-Host "copilot-proxy: shim ON -> $(Get-CopilotShimBase) (-> $(Get-CopilotBase))" } }
262	                    else { Write-Host "copilot-proxy: shim enabled; will start with the proxy." }
263	                    Write-Host "  NOTE: restart Claude Code so it picks up ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=$(Get-CopilotShimBase)"
264	                }
265	                'off' {
266	                    'off' | Set-Content $sf; Stop-CopilotShim
267	                    Write-Host "copilot-proxy: shim OFF (clients use $(Get-CopilotBase) directly)"
268	                }
269	                default {
270	                    if (Get-CopilotShimEnabled) {
271	                        $st = if (Test-CopilotShimAlive) { 'up' } else { 'down' }
272	                        Write-Host "copilot-proxy: shim ON ($st) on $(Get-CopilotShimBase)"
273	                    } else { Write-Host "copilot-proxy: shim off" }
274	                }
275	            }
276	        }
277	        { $_ -in '-h', '--help', 'help' } {
278	            Write-Host "Usage: copilot-proxy [start|stop|restart|status|doctor [--live]|logs [shim|N [gen]]|shim [on|off|status]|whoami|auth]"
279	        }
280	        default { Write-Error "copilot-proxy: unknown action '$action' (try --help)" }
281	    }
282	}
283	
284	# ------------------------------------------------------------------ doctor ----
285	function script:Invoke-CopilotDoctor {
286	    param([switch] $Live)
287	    $port = Get-CopilotPort; $pkg = Get-CopilotPkg
288	    $fail = 0; $warn = 0
289	    function OK   ($n, $m) { Write-Host ("  " + [char]0x2713 + " {0,-16} {1}" -f $n, $m) -ForegroundColor Green }
290	    function BAD  ($n, $m) { Write-Host ("  " + [char]0x2717 + " {0,-16} {1}" -f $n, $m) -ForegroundColor Red; $script:fail++ }
291	    function NOTE ($n, $m) { Write-Host ("  ! {0,-16} {1}" -f $n, $m) -ForegroundColor Yellow; $script:warn++ }
292	    function SKIP ($n, $m) { Write-Host ("  " + [char]0x00B7 + " {0,-16} {1}" -f $n, $m) }
293	    function HINT ($m)     { Write-Host ("    {0,-16} -> {1}" -f '', $m) }
294	    $script:fail = 0; $script:warn = 0
295	
296	    Write-Host "`ncopilot-proxy doctor   port $port   pkg $pkg`n"
297	
298	    Write-Host 'Prerequisites'
299	    foreach ($t in 'bun', 'node', 'uv') {
300	        $c = Get-Command $t -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
301	        if ($c) { OK $t $c.Source } elseif ($t -eq 'uv') { NOTE $t 'not found — semsearch needs it' } else { BAD $t 'not found' }
302	    }
303	
304	    Write-Host "`nAuthentication"
305	    if (Test-Path (Get-CopilotToken)) { OK 'token file' (Get-CopilotToken) } else { BAD 'token file' 'absent'; HINT 'copilot-proxy auth' }
306	
307	    Write-Host "`nProxy"
308	    if (Test-CopilotAlive) { OK 'listening' (Get-CopilotBase) } else { BAD 'listening' "nothing on port $port"; HINT 'copilot-proxy start' }
309	    if (Get-CopilotShimEnabled) {
310	        if (Test-CopilotShimAlive) { OK 'throttle shim' "up on $(Get-CopilotShimBase)" } else { BAD 'throttle shim' 'enabled but DOWN'; HINT 'copilot-proxy shim on' }
311	    } else { SKIP 'throttle shim' 'off' }
312	
313	    Write-Host "`nModels"
314	    $served = Get-CopilotServedModels
315	    if ($served -and $served.Count -gt 0) {
316	        $claude = @($served | Where-Object { $_ -match '^claude' })
317	        OK 'served' "$($served.Count) model ids"
318	        if ($claude.Count -gt 0) { OK 'claude models' "$($claude.Count) ids available" }
319	        else { BAD 'claude models' "0 of $($served.Count) — the proxy serves no Anthropic models" }
320	
321	        $pin = (Get-CopilotEffectiveModel) -split '\|', 2
322	        $model = $pin[0]; $src = $pin[1]
323	        if ($served -contains $model) { OK 'pinned model' "$model  ($src)" }
324	        else { BAD 'pinned model' "$model  ($src)"; HINT 'not served -> requests return 400 model_not_supported'; HINT 'copilot-model -l' }
325	    } else { BAD 'served' "could not fetch $(Get-CopilotBase)/v1/models" }
326	
327	    Write-Host "`nUpstream (GitHub Copilot API)"
328	    foreach ($h in 'api.enterprise.githubcopilot.com', 'api.githubcopilot.com') {
329	        try {
330	            $sw = [System.Diagnostics.Stopwatch]::StartNew()
331	            $r = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "https://$h/models" -TimeoutSec 12 -SkipHttpErrorCheck -ErrorAction Stop
332	            $sw.Stop()
333	            OK $h "HTTP $($r.StatusCode) in $([math]::Round($sw.Elapsed.TotalSeconds,2))s"
334	        } catch { BAD $h 'no response within 12s'; HINT 'connection blocked or upstream unreachable' }
335	    }
336	    SKIP '' 'HTTP 400/401 = reached (an unauthenticated probe is expected to be rejected)'
337	
338	    Write-Host "`nLive probe"
339	    if (-not $Live) { SKIP 'skipped' 'pass --live to send one real request (consumes 1 quota unit)' }
340	    elseif (-not (Test-CopilotAlive)) { SKIP 'skipped' 'proxy is not running' }
341	    elseif (-not $served) { SKIP 'skipped' 'no served model to probe with' }
342	    else {
343	        $pm = @($served | Where-Object { $_ -notmatch 'embedding' -and $_ -notmatch '\[1m\]' })[0]
344	        $body = @{ model = $pm; max_tokens = 1; messages = @(@{ role = 'user'; content = 'hi' }) } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5
345	        try {
346	            $sw = [System.Diagnostics.Stopwatch]::StartNew()
347	            $r = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "$(Get-CopilotClientBase)/v1/messages?beta=true" -Method Post `
348	                -ContentType 'application/json' -Body $body -TimeoutSec 60 -SkipHttpErrorCheck -ErrorAction Stop
349	            $sw.Stop()
350	            if ($r.StatusCode -ge 200 -and $r.StatusCode -lt 300) { OK 'round-trip' "$pm -> HTTP $($r.StatusCode) in $([math]::Round($sw.Elapsed.TotalSeconds,2))s" }
351	            else { BAD 'round-trip' "$pm -> HTTP $($r.StatusCode)"; HINT 'copilot-proxy logs 40' }
352	        } catch { BAD 'round-trip' "$pm -> request failed ($_)" }
353	    }
354	
355	    Write-Host ''
356	    if ($script:fail -gt 0) { Write-Host "$($script:fail) failed, $($script:warn) warning(s)`n"; return }
357	    Write-Host "all checks passed ($($script:warn) warning(s))`n"
358	}
359	
360	# ------------------------------------------------------------- copilot-run ----
361	function copilot-run {
362	    [CmdletBinding()]
363	    param([Parameter(ValueFromRemainingArguments)] [string[]] $Argv)
364	    if (-not $Argv -or $Argv.Count -eq 0) { Write-Error 'Usage: copilot-run <cmd> [args...]'; return }
365	    if (-not (Test-CopilotAlive)) { copilot-proxy start; if (-not (Test-CopilotAlive)) { return } }
366	    if ((Get-CopilotShimEnabled) -and -not (Test-CopilotShimAlive)) { Start-CopilotShim | Out-Null }
367	
368	    $model = Get-CopilotDefaultModel
369	    $inject = [ordered]@{
370	        ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL             = Get-CopilotClientBase
371	        ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN           = 'dummy'
372	        ANTHROPIC_MODEL                = $model
373	        ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL   = $model
374	        ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL = 'claude-sonnet-5[1m]'
375	        ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL  = 'claude-haiku-4-5'
376	        ANTHROPIC_SMALL_FAST_MODEL     = 'claude-haiku-4-5'
377	        CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC = '1'
378	    }
379	    if ($env:COPILOT_PROXY_QUIET -eq '1') {
380	        $inject.CLAUDE_CODE_ATTRIBUTION_HEADER = '0'
381	        $inject.CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_PROMPT_SUGGESTION = 'false'
382	        $inject.CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY = '0'
383	        $inject.DISABLE_NON_ESSENTIAL_MODEL_CALLS = '1'
384	    }
385	    # Scope env to the child process: set, run, restore (equivalent to `env VAR=..`).
386	    $saved = @{}
387	    foreach ($k in $inject.Keys) { $saved[$k] = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable($k); Set-Item "env:$k" $inject[$k] }
388	    try {
389	        $exe = $Argv[0]; $rest = if ($Argv.Count -gt 1) { $Argv[1..($Argv.Count - 1)] } else { @() }
390	        & $exe @rest
391	    } finally {
392	        foreach ($k in $inject.Keys) {
393	            if ($null -eq $saved[$k]) { Remove-Item "env:$k" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue }
394	            else { Set-Item "env:$k" $saved[$k] }
395	        }
396	    }
397	}
398	
399	# --------------------------------------------------------- claude-copilot -----
400	function claude-copilot {
401	    [CmdletBinding()]
402	    param([Parameter(ValueFromRemainingArguments)] [string[]] $Argv)
403	    $ss = 'auto'
404	    if ($Argv -and $Argv[0] -eq '--no-specstory') { $ss = 'never'; $Argv = $Argv[1..($Argv.Count - 1)] }
405	    elseif ($Argv -and $Argv[0] -eq '--specstory') { $Argv = $Argv[1..($Argv.Count - 1)] }
406	    elseif ($Argv -and $Argv[0] -in '-h', '--help') {
407	        Write-Host "Usage: claude-copilot [--no-specstory] [claude args...]"
408	        Write-Host "  One-off Claude Code session on the Copilot proxy. Sticky: copilot-here on"
409	        return
410	    }
411	    if ($ss -eq 'auto' -and (Get-Command specstory -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) {
412	        if ($Argv -and $Argv.Count -gt 0) { copilot-run specstory run claude -c "claude $($Argv -join ' ')" }
413	        else { copilot-run specstory run claude }
414	    } else {
415	        copilot-run claude @Argv
416	    }
417	}
418	
419	# --------------------------------------------------- claude-copilot-once ------
420	function claude-copilot-once {
421	    [CmdletBinding()]
422	    param([Parameter(ValueFromRemainingArguments)] [string[]] $Argv)
423	    if ($Argv -and $Argv[0] -in '-h', '--help') {
424	        Write-Host "Usage: claude-copilot-once [--no-specstory] [claude args...]"
425	        Write-Host "  Pin this project to the proxy, run one session, auto-unpin (even on Ctrl-C)."
426	        return
427	    }
428	    if (-not (Test-CopilotAlive)) {
429	        Write-Error "claude-copilot-once: proxy not reachable on port $(Get-CopilotPort). Start it: copilot-proxy start"; return
430	    }
431	    $wasOn = $false
432	    if (Test-Path '.claude/settings.local.json') {
433	        try { if ((Get-Content -Raw '.claude/settings.local.json' | ConvertFrom-Json).env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL) { $wasOn = $true } } catch { $null = $_ }
434	    }
435	    if (-not $wasOn) { copilot-here on }
436	    else { Write-Host "claude-copilot-once: copilot-here already ON here — leaving the pin in place on exit." }
437	    try {
438	        claude-copilot @Argv
439	    } finally {
440	        if (-not $wasOn) { copilot-here off }
441	        Write-Host "claude-copilot-once: session ended. Proxy still running on $(Get-CopilotBase)."
442	    }
443	}
444	
445	# ------------------------------------------------------------ copilot-here ----
446	$script:CopilotHereKeys = @(
447	    'ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL', 'ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN', 'ANTHROPIC_MODEL', 'ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL',
448	    'ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL', 'ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL', 'ANTHROPIC_SMALL_FAST_MODEL',
449	    'CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC', 'CLAUDE_CODE_ATTRIBUTION_HEADER',
450	    'CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_PROMPT_SUGGESTION', 'CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY', 'DISABLE_NON_ESSENTIAL_MODEL_CALLS'
451	)
452	
453	function copilot-here {
454	    [CmdletBinding()]
455	    param([Parameter(ValueFromRemainingArguments)] [string[]] $Argv)
456	    $settings = '.claude/settings.local.json'
457	    $action = if ($Argv -and $Argv.Count -ge 1) { $Argv[0] } else { 'status' }
458	    switch ($action) {
459	        'on' {
460	            New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path '.claude' | Out-Null
461	            $obj = if (Test-Path $settings) { try { Get-Content -Raw $settings | ConvertFrom-Json } catch { [pscustomobject]@{} } } else { [pscustomobject]@{} }
462	            if (-not $obj.PSObject.Properties['env']) { $obj | Add-Member -NotePropertyName env -NotePropertyValue ([pscustomobject]@{}) }
463	            $model = Get-CopilotDefaultModel
464	            $envSet = [ordered]@{
465	                ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL             = Get-CopilotPinnedBase
466	                ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN           = 'dummy'
467	                ANTHROPIC_MODEL                = $model
468	                ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL   = $model
469	                ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL = 'claude-sonnet-5[1m]'
470	                ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL  = 'claude-haiku-4-5'
471	                ANTHROPIC_SMALL_FAST_MODEL     = 'claude-haiku-4-5'
472	                CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC = '1'
473	            }
474	            if ($env:COPILOT_PROXY_QUIET -eq '1') {
475	                $envSet.CLAUDE_CODE_ATTRIBUTION_HEADER = '0'
476	                $envSet.CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_PROMPT_SUGGESTION = 'false'
477	                $envSet.CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY = '0'
478	                $envSet.DISABLE_NON_ESSENTIAL_MODEL_CALLS = '1'
479	            }
480	            foreach ($k in $envSet.Keys) {
481	                if ($obj.env.PSObject.Properties[$k]) { $obj.env.$k = $envSet[$k] }
482	                else { $obj.env | Add-Member -NotePropertyName $k -NotePropertyValue $envSet[$k] }
483	            }
484	            $obj | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10 | Set-Content -Path $settings -Encoding utf8
485	            # Belt-and-braces gitignore via .git/info/exclude (un-anchored **/ form).
486	            $gitDir = git rev-parse --git-dir 2>$null
487	            if ($gitDir) {
488	                git check-ignore -q $settings 2>$null
489	                if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
490	                    $exclude = Join-Path $gitDir 'info/exclude'
491	                    Add-Content -Path $exclude -Value '**/.claude/settings.local.json'
492	                }
493	            }
494	            Write-Host "copilot-here: ON — $settings pins Claude Code to $(Get-CopilotBase) (model: $model)"
495	            if (-not (Test-CopilotAlive)) { Write-Host "  WARNING proxy not running — start it: copilot-proxy start" }
496	        }
497	        'off' {
498	            if (-not (Test-Path $settings)) { Write-Host "copilot-here: already off (no $settings)"; return }
499	            try { $obj = Get-Content -Raw $settings | ConvertFrom-Json } catch { Write-Error "copilot-here: $settings is not valid JSON"; return }
500	            if ($obj.env) {
501	                foreach ($k in $script:CopilotHereKeys) { if ($obj.env.PSObject.Properties[$k]) { $obj.env.PSObject.Properties.Remove($k) } }
502	                if (-not $obj.env.PSObject.Properties) { $obj.PSObject.Properties.Remove('env') }
503	            }
504	            if (-not $obj.PSObject.Properties.Name) {
505	                Remove-Item $settings -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
506	                Write-Host "copilot-here: OFF — removed $settings (it held only proxy config)"
507	            } else {
508	                $obj | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10 | Set-Content -Path $settings -Encoding utf8
509	                Write-Host "copilot-here: OFF — proxy env removed from $settings (other content kept)"
510	            }
511	        }
512	        'status' {
513	            if (Test-Path $settings) {
514	                try { $obj = Get-Content -Raw $settings | ConvertFrom-Json } catch { $obj = $null }
515	                if ($obj.env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL) {
516	                    Write-Host "copilot-here: ON  (base: $($obj.env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL), model: $($obj.env.ANTHROPIC_MODEL))"
517	                    if (-not (Test-CopilotAlive)) { Write-Host "  WARNING proxy not running — start it: copilot-proxy start" }
518	                    return
519	                }
520	            }
521	            Write-Host "copilot-here: off  (enable: copilot-here on; one-off: claude-copilot)"
522	        }
523	        default { Write-Host "Usage: copilot-here [on|off|status]" }
524	    }
525	}
526	
527	# ----------------------------------------------------------- copilot-model ----
528	function copilot-model {
529	    [CmdletBinding()]
530	    param([Parameter(ValueFromRemainingArguments)] [string[]] $Argv)
531	    $settings = '.claude/settings.local.json'
532	    $statef = Get-CopilotModelState
533	    $arg = if ($Argv -and $Argv.Count -ge 1) { $Argv[0] } else { '' }
534	
535	    $target = 'state'
536	    if (Test-Path $settings) {
537	        try { if ((Get-Content -Raw $settings | ConvertFrom-Json).env.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL) { $target = 'local' } } catch { $null = $_ }
538	    }
539	
540	    function script:_ModelList {
541	        try {
542	            $r = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$(Get-CopilotBase)/v1/models" -TimeoutSec 3 -ErrorAction Stop
543	            return ($r.data.id | Sort-Object)
544	        } catch {
545	            Write-Host "copilot-model: proxy not reachable — showing fallback list"
546	            return @('claude-opus-4-8', 'claude-opus-4-7', 'claude-sonnet-5', 'claude-haiku-4-5')
547	        }
548	    }
549	    function script:_ModelCurrent {
550	        if ($target -eq 'local') { (Get-Content -Raw $settings | ConvertFrom-Json).env.ANTHROPIC_MODEL }
551	        else { Get-CopilotDefaultModel }
552	    }
553	
554	    switch ($arg) {
555	        { $_ -in '-l', '--list' } { _ModelList; return }
556	        { $_ -in '-c', '--current' } {
557	            if ($target -eq 'local') { Write-Host "$(_ModelCurrent)  (project: $settings)" } else { Write-Host "$(_ModelCurrent)  (global: $statef)" }
558	            return
559	        }
560	        { $_ -in '-h', '--help' } { Write-Host "Usage: copilot-model [<model-id>|-l|-c]"; return }
561	    }
562	
563	    $models = _ModelList
564	    $resolved = ''
565	    if (-not $arg) {
566	        if (-not (Get-Command fzf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { Write-Error "copilot-model: pass a model id (fzf not found). Try: copilot-model -l"; return }
567	        $want = $models | fzf --prompt='model> ' --height=40% --reverse --header="current: $(_ModelCurrent)"
568	        if (-not $want) { Write-Host 'cancelled'; return }
569	        $resolved = $want
570	    } else {
571	        $suffix = ''; $base = $arg
572	        if ($arg -match '\[1m\]$') { $suffix = '[1m]'; $base = $arg -replace '\[1m\]$', '' }
573	        $norm = $base -replace '\.', '-'
574	        foreach ($cand in @($base, "claude-$base", $norm, "claude-$norm")) {
575	            if ($models -contains $cand) { $resolved = $cand; break }
576	        }
577	        if (-not $resolved) {
578	            $hits = @($models | Where-Object { $_ -like "*$norm*" })
579	            if ($hits.Count -eq 1) { $resolved = $hits[0] }
580	            else { Write-Error "copilot-model: '$arg' did not match a unique model. Try: copilot-model -l"; return }
581	        }
582	        $resolved = "$resolved$suffix"
583	    }
584	
585	    $old = _ModelCurrent
586	    if ($old -eq $resolved) { Write-Host "copilot-model: already using $resolved (no change)"; return }
587	
588	    if ($target -eq 'local') {
589	        $obj = Get-Content -Raw $settings | ConvertFrom-Json
590	        $obj.env.ANTHROPIC_MODEL = $resolved
591	        $obj.env.ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL = $resolved
592	        $obj | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10 | Set-Content -Path $settings -Encoding utf8
593	        Write-Host "copilot-model: $old -> $resolved  (project: $settings)"
594	        Write-Host "  restart Claude Code to apply (exit, then: claude -c)"
595	    } else {
596	        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path (Split-Path $statef) | Out-Null
597	        $resolved | Set-Content $statef
598	        Write-Host "copilot-model: $old -> $resolved  (global: $statef)"
599	    }
600	}
601	
602	# ----------------------------------------------------------- copilot-embed ----
603	function copilot-embed {
604	    [CmdletBinding()]
605	    param([Parameter(ValueFromRemainingArguments)] [string[]] $Argv)
606	    $model = if ($env:AICAP_EMBED_MODEL) { $env:AICAP_EMBED_MODEL } else { 'text-embedding-3-small' }
607	    $wantJson = $false; $doList = $false
608	    $rest = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]::new()
609	    for ($i = 0; $i -lt $Argv.Count; $i++) {
610	        switch -Regex ($Argv[$i]) {
611	            '^(-m|--model)$' { $model = $Argv[++$i] }
612	            '^--model=' { $model = $Argv[$i] -replace '^--model=', '' }
613	            '^--json$' { $wantJson = $true }
614	            '^(-l|--list)$' { $doList = $true }
615	            '^(-h|--help)$' { Write-Host "Usage: copilot-embed [--model M] [--json] [TEXT | -]`n       copilot-embed -l"; return }
616	            default { $rest.Add($Argv[$i]) }
617	        }
618	    }
619	    if (-not (Test-CopilotAlive)) { copilot-proxy start; if (-not (Test-CopilotAlive)) { return } }
620	
621	    if ($doList) {
622	        try { (Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$(Get-CopilotBase)/v1/models" -TimeoutSec 3).data.id | Where-Object { $_ -match 'embed' } | Sort-Object } catch { $null = $_ }
623	        return
624	    }
625	
626	    $text = if ($rest.Count -gt 0 -and $rest[0] -ne '-') { $rest -join ' ' } elseif (-not [Console]::IsInputRedirected) { Write-Error "copilot-embed: no text — pass an arg or pipe stdin"; return } else { [Console]::In.ReadToEnd() }
627	    if (-not $text) { Write-Error "copilot-embed: empty input"; return }
628	
629	    # input MUST be an array (scalar 400s — fork issue #100).
630	    $payload = if ($model) { @{ model = $model; input = @($text) } } else { @{ input = @($text) } }
631	    try {
632	        $resp = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$(Get-CopilotBase)/v1/embeddings" -Method Post -ContentType 'application/json' `
633	            -Body ($payload | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5) -TimeoutSec 60 -ErrorAction Stop
634	    } catch { Write-Error "copilot-embed: request failed — is the proxy up? ($_)"; return }
635	
636	    if ($wantJson) { $resp | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 8; return }
637	    $vec = $resp.data[0].embedding
638	    if (-not $vec) { Write-Error "copilot-embed: empty embedding"; return }
639	    $vec | ConvertTo-Json -Compress
640	}
641	
642	# ------------------------------------------------------------- semsearch ------
643	function semsearch {
644	    [CmdletBinding()]
645	    param([Parameter(ValueFromRemainingArguments)] [string[]] $Argv)
646	    if (-not $Argv -or $Argv[0] -in '-h', '--help') {
647	        Write-Host "Usage: semsearch index [PATH...]        # build/refresh an index"
648	        Write-Host "       semsearch <QUERY> [-k N] [--corpus PATH]"
649	        if (-not $Argv) { return }
650	        return
651	    }
652	    if (-not (Get-Command uv -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { Write-Error "semsearch: uv is required (scoop install uv)"; return }
653	    $src = chezmoi source-path 2>$null
654	    if (-not $src) { Write-Error "semsearch: could not resolve chezmoi source-path"; return }
655	    $script = Join-Path $src 'scripts/semsearch.py'
656	    if (-not (Test-Path $script)) { Write-Error "semsearch: $script not found (run 'chezmoi apply' after a git pull)"; return }
657	    if (-not (Test-CopilotAlive)) { copilot-proxy start; if (-not (Test-CopilotAlive)) { return } }
658	    $env:COPILOT_EMBED_BASE = Get-CopilotBase
659	    uv run --script $script @Argv
660	}
661	
662	Export-ModuleMember -Function 'copilot-proxy', 'copilot-run', 'claude-copilot', 'claude-copilot-once',
663	    'copilot-here', 'copilot-model', 'copilot-embed', 'semsearch'
664
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	#Requires -Version 7
2	# run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1 — install the toolset with scoop (CLI) +
3	# winget (GUI). Idempotent: only missing packages are installed. This script is
4	# the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for what gets installed; re-runs whenever the
5	# rendered content (i.e. any toggle below) changes.
6	#
7	# Toggles (from .chezmoi.toml.tmpl):
8	#   installCodingAgents  = {{ .installCodingAgents }}
9	#   installWindowsApps   = {{ .installWindowsApps }}
10	#   installUtilityApps   = {{ .installUtilityApps }}
11	#   installGamingApps    = {{ .installGamingApps }}
12	#   installExtraRuntimes = {{ .installExtraRuntimes }}
13	#   installMediaTools    = {{ .installMediaTools }}
14	#   installLlmTools      = {{ .installLlmTools }}
15	#   installTunnelTools   = {{ .installTunnelTools }}
16	#   installIacTools      = {{ .installIacTools }}
17	#   useChineseMirror     = {{ .useChineseMirror }}
18	#   managedMachine       = {{ .managedMachine }}
19	
20	# Resilient by design: individual package failures are collected and reported at
21	# the end, never fatal — one bad manifest must not abort the rest of the install
22	# (nor the rest of `chezmoi apply`). Mirrors ansible allow-partial-failure.
23	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'
24	if (Test-Path variable:PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference) { $PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference = $false }
25	$script:Failures = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]::new()
26	
27	# UTF-8 console so winget's localized output isn't mojibake on zh-* Windows.
28	try { [Console]::OutputEncoding = [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false) } catch { $null = $_ }
29	{{ if .useChineseMirror }}
30	# China (GFW) mirrors for install-time package fetches.
31	$env:PIP_INDEX_URL = 'https://pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/simple'
32	$env:UV_DEFAULT_INDEX = 'https://pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/simple'
33	$env:npm_config_registry = 'https://registry.npmmirror.com'
34	$env:GOPROXY = 'https://goproxy.cn,direct'
35	$env:MISE_NODE_MIRROR_URL = 'https://npmmirror.com/mirrors/node/'
36	{{ end }}
37	
38	function Info($m) { Write-Host "==> $m" -ForegroundColor Cyan }
39	function Have($n) { [bool](Get-Command $n -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) }
40	function Register-Failure($what) { $script:Failures.Add($what); Write-Warning "FAILED (skipped): $what" }
41	
42	# --- scoop -----------------------------------------------------------------
43	function Test-Admin {
44	    # $true when this pwsh is running elevated (member of the Administrators role).
45	    try {
46	        $id = [Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent()
47	        ([Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal]$id).IsInRole([Security.Principal.WindowsBuiltInRole]::Administrator)
48	    } catch { $false }
49	}
50	
51	function Ensure-Scoop {
52	    if (-not (Have scoop)) {
53	        # get.scoop.sh returns install.ps1's text. Piping it to Invoke-Expression
54	        # works in a normal console, but an ELEVATED one is refused ("Running the
55	        # installer as administrator is disabled by default") unless -RunAsAdmin is
56	        # passed. Some dev boxes always open the shell as admin, so detect that and
57	        # forward the flag. We build a scriptblock from the fetched text so we can
58	        # pass args -- equivalent to `iex "& {$(irm get.scoop.sh)} -RunAsAdmin"`.
59	        $installer = [scriptblock]::Create((Invoke-RestMethod get.scoop.sh))
60	        if (Test-Admin) {
61	            Info 'scoop not found — installing (elevated console: passing -RunAsAdmin)'
62	            & $installer -RunAsAdmin
63	        } else {
64	            Info 'scoop not found — installing (user-scoped, no admin)'
65	            & $installer
66	        }
67	    }
68	    foreach ($b in 'main', 'extras', 'versions', 'nerd-fonts') {
69	        scoop bucket add $b *> $null
70	    }
71	}
72	
73	function Get-ScoopInstalled {
74	    try { return @((scoop export | ConvertFrom-Json).apps.name) } catch { return @() }
75	}
76	
77	function Scoop-Install([string[]]$apps) {
78	    if (-not $apps) { return }
79	    # Wrap in @() so a lone survivor stays an array. Without it PowerShell
80	    # unwraps a single match to a bare string, and `scoop install @missing`
81	    # then splats it one CHARACTER per arg => "Couldn't find manifest for 'm'"
82	    # when only "mise" was missing.
83	    $missing = @($apps | Where-Object { ($_ -split '/')[-1] -notin (Get-ScoopInstalled) })
84	    if (-not $missing) { Info 'scoop: all requested tools already present'; return }
85	    Info "scoop install: $($missing -join ', ')"
86	    try { scoop install @missing } catch { Write-Warning "scoop batch error: $_" }
87	    # Anything still absent after the batch = failed (scoop keeps going past a bad one).
88	    $after = Get-ScoopInstalled
89	    foreach ($app in $missing) {
90	        $name = ($app -split '/')[-1]
91	        if ($name -notin $after) { Register-Failure "scoop:$name" }
92	    }
93	}
94	
95	# --- winget ----------------------------------------------------------------
96	function Winget-Install([string]$id) {
97	    if (-not (Have winget)) { Register-Failure "winget:$id (winget unavailable)"; return }
98	    winget list --id $id -e --accept-source-agreements *> $null
99	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { return }   # already installed
100	    Info "winget install $id"
101	    winget install --id $id -e --silent --scope user `
102	        --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
103	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
104	        # some packages don't support --scope user; retry machine scope.
105	        winget install --id $id -e --silent `
106	            --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
107	    }
108	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "winget:$id" }
109	}
110	
111	# Microsoft Store packages (installed by Store product id via the msstore
112	# source) — used for apps with no community winget manifest, e.g. the official
113	# ChatGPT / Codex desktop apps. msstore ignores --scope, so we don't pass it.
114	function Winget-InstallStore([string]$id) {
115	    if (-not (Have winget)) { Register-Failure "msstore:$id (winget unavailable)"; return }
116	    winget list --id $id -e --accept-source-agreements *> $null
117	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { return }   # already installed
118	    Info "winget (msstore) install $id"
119	    winget install --id $id -e --source msstore --silent `
120	        --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
121	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "msstore:$id" }
122	}
123	
124	# --- npm CLI agents --------------------------------------------------------
125	function Npm-InstallGlobal([string[]]$pkgs) {
126	    if (-not (Have npm)) { foreach ($p in $pkgs) { Register-Failure "npm:$p (npm unavailable)" }; return }
127	    foreach ($p in $pkgs) {
128	        Info "npm i -g $p"
129	        npm install -g $p 2>&1 | Out-Host
130	        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "npm:$p" }
131	    }
132	}
133	
134	# ===========================================================================
135	Ensure-Scoop
136	
137	# Core CLI toolset — the shell baseline, always installed.
138	Scoop-Install @(
139	    'git', 'gh', 'glab', '7zip', 'make', 'just',
140	    'starship', 'zoxide', 'fzf', 'fd', 'ripgrep', 'bat', 'eza', 'jq',
141	    'delta', 'lazygit', 'neovim', 'yazi', 'btop', 'television', 'tlrc',
142	    'uv', 'gnupg',
143	    # Runtimes are NATIVE via scoop, not mise: mise's activate/shims are
144	    # unreliable on Windows and it has no windows-arm64 bun build. node + bun
145	    # here; Python via uv (below); go/rust/ruby under Extra runtimes.
146	    # copilot-proxy needs bun/bunx.
147	    'nodejs-lts', 'bun',
148	    # LazyVim needs a C compiler for tree-sitter parsers (zig), the tree-sitter
149	    # CLI the nvim-treesitter `main` branch requires, and a clipboard provider so
150	    # `unnamedplus` yanks reach the Windows clipboard (win32yank).
151	    'zig', 'tree-sitter', 'win32yank'
152	)
153	
154	# On Windows-on-ARM, scoop's neovim is amd64 (runs under x64 emulation) while
155	# zig installs arm64-native, so zig-built tree-sitter parsers are the wrong
156	# arch for nvim. Add an amd64 gcc that matches the emulated nvim.
157	if ([System.Runtime.InteropServices.RuntimeInformation]::OSArchitecture -eq 'Arm64') {
158	    Info 'ARM64 host: adding amd64 gcc for nvim-treesitter'
159	    Scoop-Install @('gcc')
160	}
161	
162	# gh-dash: GitHub PR/issue dashboard TUI (`gh dash`). It's a gh extension, not a
163	# scoop package, so it needs gh to be authenticated -- this no-ops on a fresh
164	# machine until `gh auth login`. run_onchange only re-fires when this script
165	# changes, so after authenticating, install by hand (`gh extension install
166	# dlvhdr/gh-dash`) or force a reapply (`chezmoi state delete-bucket
167	# --bucket=entryState`). gh-notify / gh-select from the Unix repo are bash-only
168	# extensions and are intentionally skipped on Windows.
169	if (Have gh) {
170	    gh auth status *> $null
171	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
172	        $ghExts = gh extension list 2>$null | Out-String
173	        if ($ghExts -notmatch 'dlvhdr/gh-dash') {
174	            Info 'gh extension install dlvhdr/gh-dash'
175	            gh extension install dlvhdr/gh-dash
176	            if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'gh-ext:gh-dash' }
177	        }
178	    } else {
179	        Info 'gh not authenticated -- skipping gh-dash (gh auth login, then reapply)'
180	    }
181	}
182	
183	# node/bun come from scoop above (on ~/scoop/shims, reliably on PATH). We do NOT
184	# use mise here -- see the runtimes note in the core scoop list.
185	
186	# Default Python, uv-managed. --default also creates unversioned python/python3
187	# (not just python3.x) in UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR (~/.local/bin, on PATH via 00_env
188	# ahead of the Store's python.exe app-alias). Needs uv >= 0.8.
189	if (Have uv) {
190	    Info 'uv python install --default'
191	    $env:UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR = Join-Path $HOME '.local\bin'
192	    uv python install --default 2>&1 | Out-Host
193	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'uv:python' }
194	}
195	
196	# Make PSGallery usable non-interactively before Install-Module (NuGet provider
197	# + trust) so the fzf key bindings / audio cmdlets install reliably.
198	try {
199	    if (-not (Get-PackageProvider -Name NuGet -ListAvailable -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) {
200	        Install-PackageProvider -Name NuGet -Scope CurrentUser -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Out-Null
201	    }
202	    if ((Get-PSRepository -Name PSGallery -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).InstallationPolicy -ne 'Trusted') {
203	        Set-PSRepository -Name PSGallery -InstallationPolicy Trusted -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

207	# PSReadLine ships with pwsh; PSFzf powers the fzf key bindings in profile.d

216	    Install-Module AudioDeviceCmdlets -Scope CurrentUser -Force -AcceptLicense -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
217	}
```
