```md
# Fresh-machine setup fixes + OpenSSH server

## Context

Setting up `hanruzhou`'s new (OneDrive-KFM, Microsoft-corp) Windows box surfaced
several defects and one feature request. All are independent and should land as
**separate commits**, but are planned together because they came from one setup log.

1. **OneDrive `$PROFILE` redirect is fragile** (the core request). On a machine
   where `Documents` is OneDrive-redirected, `run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1`
   writes a *stub* at the real `$PROFILE` that dot-sources the **literal**
   `~/Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1` — which need not exist
   there under KFM. Result: `The term '...\Documents\PowerShell\...ps1' is not
   recognized`. The managed loader is location-independent (only references
   `~/.config/powershell`), so the stub-pointer indirection is unnecessary and
   should be replaced by writing the loader **content** directly.
2. **pwsh startup takes 8827 ms.** `10_tools.ps1` cold-spawns 5–6 init binaries on
   every shell and does 3 full `PSModulePath` scans (slow under OneDrive hydration).
3. **`uv python install --default` fails** on uv 0.11+ (`--default` moved behind
   `--preview`). Stale "needs uv >= 0.8" comment; command echoed in 4 docs files.
4. **msstore ChatGPT ID is wrong** — mislabeled, not just dead (see IDs below).
5. **New request: install/enable the OpenSSH server (sshd).**

Verified facts from exploration:
- CI (`.github/workflows/windows.yml:100-113`) auto-discovers every `*.ps1.tmpl`
  via `Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Filter *.ps1.tmpl` and render+parse-checks it — a
  renamed/added `.tmpl` needs **no** manual CI list edit.
- `{{ include }}`-into-single-quoted-here-string pattern already exists in
  `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_20_editor_overlays.ps1.tmpl:10-15`.
- `run_onchange` re-fires when the **rendered** script content changes, so embedding
  the loader via `{{ include }}` makes the redirect re-run whenever the loader edits.
- `$env:XDG_CACHE_HOME` is set in `00_env.ps1:18` (runs before `10_tools.ps1`).
- Loader source is verbatim `Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1`
  (no `dot_`/`.tmpl`, not in `.chezmoiignore`, no `.chezmoiroot`).

---

## Workstream 1 — OneDrive `$PROFILE` redirect (core)

**Convert** `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1`
→ `run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1.tmpl` (git mv/rename).

Change the write logic (currently `.ps1:47-51`): when `$realFull -ine $managedFull`,
instead of writing a stub that dot-sources `$managedFull`, write the **full managed
loader content** to `$real`, with a marker header line so we can detect our own copy.

- Embed the loader once, mirroring `run_onchange_after_20_editor_overlays.ps1.tmpl:10-15`:
  \```powershell
  $loader = @'
  {{ include "Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1" }}
  '@
  \```
  Single-quoted here-string is required — the loader contains `$HOME`, `$env:...`,
  `$($fragment.Name)` that must stay literal.
- Written file = marker line + loader body, e.g. first line
  `# Managed by chezmoi (dotfiles-windows) — OneDrive KFM redirect copy. Overrides: ~/.config/powershell/local.ps1`.
- **Keep** the existing once-only backup to
  `~/.dotfiles-backup/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1.onedrive.bak` and the marker-based
  "is this already our copy?" skip (`.ps1:33-45`) — update the marker string used by both.
- Add an idempotency guard: if `$real` already equals the intended content, skip the
  write (avoid churn; run_onchange already gates cross-apply re-runs).
- No-redirect path (`$realFull -ieq $managedFull`) stays a no-op; chezmoi manages the
  literal file directly. Keep `$ErrorActionPreference='Continue'` + try/catch (never abort).

**Why content, not pointer:** removes all dependency on the literal `~/Documents`
copy existing; eliminates any self-source recursion risk; and (via `{{ include }}`
re-hashing) future loader edits now propagate to the OneDrive copy automatically —
a latent bug the current plain-`.ps1` stub has (it never updates on loader change).

Files: `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1` (→ `.tmpl`).

---

## Workstream 2 — pwsh startup performance

Primary lever: **cache each tool's init output** to `$XDG_CACHE_HOME/pwsh-init/`,
keyed on the tool exe's `LastWriteTimeUtc` (auto-busts on scoop upgrade), so warm
shells dot-source a static file instead of cold-spawning the binary.

In `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/10_tools.ps1`:
- Add a helper `Import-CachedInit -Name <n> -Exe <exe> -Generate { ... }` at the top:
  resolves the exe, compares its mtime to a saved stamp, regenerates
  `pwsh-init/<n>.ps1` on miss/staleness, then dot-sources it. Guard on
  `Get-Command $Exe`; wrap generation in try/catch (never error the prompt).
- Route the 5 spawns through it:
  - starship (`:5-7`), zoxide (`:10-12`), atuin (`:18-20`), direnv (`:35-37`),
    tv (`:43-47`, keeping the `power-shell`→`powershell` fallback inside the generator).
  These emit **static** init text (no per-session state), so caching is safe.
- Replace `Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name X` (full PSModulePath scan) with direct
  `Import-Module X -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue` + a cheap `Get-Module -Name X`
  (loaded-only) check, in three places: PSFzf (`10_tools.ps1:28-31`),
  Copilot (`40_copilot.ps1:4-8`), PSReadLine (`90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl:3-4`).

Minor / optional (call out, low priority):
- `98_tv_cache.ps1` regenerates `aliases.txt` every start — add a "skip if content
  unchanged" or mtime guard.
- The loader has no interactive gate; a non-interactive early-return is low value
  (chezmoi runs scripts with `-NoProfile`) — leave unless measurement says otherwise.

**Measure first:** 8827 ms is partly first-run cold cost (Defender scanning freshly
downloaded exes + OneDrive hydration). After a couple of warm `Measure-Command { pwsh
-NoLogo -Command exit }` runs, the steady-state number tells us how much caching buys.

Files: `10_tools.ps1`, `40_copilot.ps1`, `90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl`, `98_tv_cache.ps1`.

---

## Workstream 3 — `uv python install --default`

In `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl:190-192`: add `--preview`
(`uv python install --default --preview`) and update the stale comment at `:188`
("Needs uv >= 0.8" → the flag now requires `--preview` on uv 0.8+/0.11+).

Docs mirror (same commit): drop-in the `--preview` form in `docs/tools.md:38`,
`docs/rationale.md:55`, `docs/tools.zh-TW.md:37`, `docs/rationale.zh-TW.md:47-48`.

---

## Workstream 4 — msstore ChatGPT ID (verification-gated)

Confirmed via Store listings: `9NT1R1C2HH7J` = **"ChatGPT Classic"** (fails / not the
app we want), `9PLM9XGG6VKS` = the real OpenAI **"ChatGPT"** (installs). Our script
labels them backwards at `run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl:272-274`.

Recommended change (after a `winget search --source msstore ChatGPT` on the box to
confirm region availability): drop the dead `9NT1R1C2HH7J`, keep `9PLM9XGG6VKS`
relabeled `# ChatGPT (OpenAI)`. Codex CLI is already covered by npm `@openai/codex`
(coding-agents block), so no separate "Codex" Store app is needed — if one is wanted,
look up its live ID rather than reusing `9PLM9XGG6VKS`.

Docs mirror: `docs/tools.md:57-58` + `docs/tools.zh-TW.md:57-58`.

---

## Workstream 5 — OpenSSH server (sshd)  [NEW]

Add an **opt-in, admin-gated, off-by-default** feature to install + enable the
Microsoft OpenSSH Server.

⚠️ **Caveat to confirm:** this opens an inbound listener (TCP 22) and enables a
system service — on a **corporate/managed** machine this may violate security policy
or be blocked. Default the toggle **off**; do not force it on. (Open decision: whether
to also hard-skip when `managedMachine` is true, like Tailscale/Grammarly — leaning
*no*, so you can knowingly opt in on the corp box, but with a printed warning.)

- **New init prompt** in `.chezmoi.toml.tmpl` via `promptBoolOnce`, default `false`.
  Proposed exact text (no `=`, per invariant #1):
  `Install and enable the OpenSSH server / sshd (needs admin, opens inbound port 22)?`
- **New dedicated script** `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_40_openssh_server.ps1.tmpl`,
  gated `{{ if .installSshServer }}` (separate from the user-scoped package installer
  because it needs admin + touches services/firewall — mirrors the dedicated
  `run_onchange_after_30_windows_terminal.ps1`). Fault-tolerant (`Continue`, never abort):
  - If not elevated (reuse a `Test-Admin` like `bootstrap.ps1:36-42`): print guidance
    to run `just enable-sshd` from an elevated pwsh, then return.
  - `Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0`
  - `Set-Service sshd -StartupType Automatic; Start-Service sshd`
  - Ensure firewall rule `OpenSSH-Server-In-TCP` (create if missing).
  - Optional: set pwsh as sshd `DefaultShell` (HKLM; admin).
- **`just enable-sshd` recipe** in `justfile` as the elevated fallback (mirrors
  `just upgrade-scoop`), running the same commands.
- **Invariant #1 (same commit):** add `--promptBool "<exact prompt text>=false"` to
  `.github/workflows/windows.yml` init flags, matching the prompt text verbatim.

---

Both new issues are real and I found their root causes:

- **Alacritty:** config uses `Hack Nerd Font Mono` (`AppData/Roaming/alacritty/alacritty.toml.tmpl:18-21`) — the *same* string Windows Terminal resolves fine. The likely culprit is Alacritty's well-known Windows limitation: it reads only **machine-wide (HKLM)** fonts, while scoop's `nerd-fonts` bucket registers Hack-NF-Mono **per-user (HKCU)** — WT/WezTerm see HKCU fonts, Alacritty doesn't.
- **WezTerm:** there is **no WezTerm config in the repo at all** — it's installed as the tmux-replacement but ships no `wezterm.lua`, so it falls back to `cmd.exe`.

1	# Fresh-machine setup fixes + OpenSSH server
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Setting up `hanruzhou`'s new (OneDrive-KFM, Microsoft-corp) Windows box surfaced
6	several defects and one feature request. All are independent and should land as
7	**separate commits**, but are planned together because they came from one setup log.
8	
9	1. **OneDrive `$PROFILE` redirect is fragile** (the core request). On a machine
10	   where `Documents` is OneDrive-redirected, `run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1`
11	   writes a *stub* at the real `$PROFILE` that dot-sources the **literal**
12	   `~/Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1` — which need not exist
13	   there under KFM. Result: `The term '...\Documents\PowerShell\...ps1' is not
14	   recognized`. The managed loader is location-independent (only references
15	   `~/.config/powershell`), so the stub-pointer indirection is unnecessary and
16	   should be replaced by writing the loader **content** directly.
17	2. **pwsh startup takes 8827 ms.** `10_tools.ps1` cold-spawns 5–6 init binaries on
18	   every shell and does 3 full `PSModulePath` scans (slow under OneDrive hydration).
19	3. **`uv python install --default` fails** on uv 0.11+ (`--default` moved behind
20	   `--preview`). Stale "needs uv >= 0.8" comment; command echoed in 4 docs files.
21	4. **msstore ChatGPT ID is wrong** — mislabeled, not just dead (see IDs below).
22	5. **New request: install/enable the OpenSSH server (sshd).**
23	6. **Alacritty can't find `Hack Nerd Font Mono`** (Windows Terminal can) — the font
24	   is registered per-user (HKCU) by scoop's nerd-fonts bucket, and Alacritty on
25	   Windows reads only machine-wide (HKLM) fonts.
26	7. **WezTerm opens `cmd.exe`, not pwsh** — the repo ships no WezTerm config at all,
27	   so `default_prog` falls back to DOS.
28	
29	Verified facts from exploration:
30	- CI (`.github/workflows/windows.yml:100-113`) auto-discovers every `*.ps1.tmpl`
31	  via `Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Filter *.ps1.tmpl` and render+parse-checks it — a
32	  renamed/added `.tmpl` needs **no** manual CI list edit.
33	- `{{ include }}`-into-single-quoted-here-string pattern already exists in
34	  `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_20_editor_overlays.ps1.tmpl:10-15`.
35	- `run_onchange` re-fires when the **rendered** script content changes, so embedding
36	  the loader via `{{ include }}` makes the redirect re-run whenever the loader edits.
37	- `$env:XDG_CACHE_HOME` is set in `00_env.ps1:18` (runs before `10_tools.ps1`).
38	- Loader source is verbatim `Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1`
39	  (no `dot_`/`.tmpl`, not in `.chezmoiignore`, no `.chezmoiroot`).

43	## Workstream 1 — OneDrive `$PROFILE` redirect (core)
44	
45	**Convert** `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1`
46	→ `run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1.tmpl` (git mv/rename).
47	
48	Change the write logic (currently `.ps1:47-51`): when `$realFull -ine $managedFull`,
49	instead of writing a stub that dot-sources `$managedFull`, write the **full managed
50	loader content** to `$real`, with a marker header line so we can detect our own copy.
51	
52	- Embed the loader once, mirroring `run_onchange_after_20_editor_overlays.ps1.tmpl:10-15`:
53	  ```powershell
54	  $loader = @'
55	  {{ include "Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1" }}
56	  '@
57	  ```
58	  Single-quoted here-string is required — the loader contains `$HOME`, `$env:...`,
59	  `$($fragment.Name)` that must stay literal.
60	- Written file = marker line + loader body, e.g. first line

62	- **Keep** the existing once-only backup to

66	  write (avoid churn; run_onchange already gates cross-apply re-runs).
67	- No-redirect path (`$realFull -ieq $managedFull`) stays a no-op; chezmoi manages the
68	  literal file directly. Keep `$ErrorActionPreference='Continue'` + try/catch (never abort).
69	
70	**Why content, not pointer:** removes all dependency on the literal `~/Documents`
71	copy existing; eliminates any self-source recursion risk; and (via `{{ include }}`
72	re-hashing) future loader edits now propagate to the OneDrive copy automatically —
73	a latent bug the current plain-`.ps1` stub has (it never updates on loader change).

79	## Workstream 2 — pwsh startup performance
80	
81	Primary lever: **cache each tool's init output** to `$XDG_CACHE_HOME/pwsh-init/`,
82	keyed on the tool exe's `LastWriteTimeUtc` (auto-busts on scoop upgrade), so warm
83	shells dot-source a static file instead of cold-spawning the binary.
84	
85	In `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/10_tools.ps1`:
86	- Add a helper `Import-CachedInit -Name <n> -Exe <exe> -Generate { ... }` at the top:
87	  resolves the exe, compares its mtime to a saved stamp, regenerates
88	  `pwsh-init/<n>.ps1` on miss/staleness, then dot-sources it. Guard on
89	  `Get-Command $Exe`; wrap generation in try/catch (never error the prompt).
90	- Route the 5 spawns through it:
91	  - starship (`:5-7`), zoxide (`:10-12`), atuin (`:18-20`), direnv (`:35-37`),
92	    tv (`:43-47`, keeping the `power-shell`→`powershell` fallback inside the generator).
93	  These emit **static** init text (no per-session state), so caching is safe.
94	- Replace `Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name X` (full PSModulePath scan) with direct
95	  `Import-Module X -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue` + a cheap `Get-Module -Name X`
96	  (loaded-only) check, in three places: PSFzf (`10_tools.ps1:28-31`),
97	  Copilot (`40_copilot.ps1:4-8`), PSReadLine (`90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl:3-4`).
98	
99	Minor / optional (call out, low priority):
100	- `98_tv_cache.ps1` regenerates `aliases.txt` every start — add a "skip if content
101	  unchanged" or mtime guard.
102	- The loader has no interactive gate; a non-interactive early-return is low value
103	  (chezmoi runs scripts with `-NoProfile`) — leave unless measurement says otherwise.
104	
105	**Measure first:** 8827 ms is partly first-run cold cost (Defender scanning freshly
106	downloaded exes + OneDrive hydration). After a couple of warm `Measure-Command { pwsh
107	-NoLogo -Command exit }` runs, the steady-state number tells us how much caching buys.

171	## Workstream 6 — Alacritty Nerd Font (verification-gated)
172	
173	Root cause (to confirm on the box): Alacritty on Windows enumerates only the
174	**machine-wide** font collection, so a Nerd Font registered **per-user** by scoop is
175	invisible to it — while Windows Terminal (which resolves the *same* `Hack Nerd Font
176	Mono` string, `run_onchange_after_30_windows_terminal.ps1:36`) and WezTerm both see
177	HKCU fonts. The Alacritty config family string (`alacritty.toml.tmpl:18-21`) is
178	correct and needs **no** change.
179	
180	**Diagnose first (Windows box):**
181	```powershell
182	[System.Drawing.FontFamily]::Families.Name | Select-String Hack   # exact family name Alacritty would match
183	Get-ItemProperty 'HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Fonts' | Format-List *Hack*
184	Get-ItemProperty 'HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Fonts' | Format-List *Hack*
185	```
186	- If `Hack*` appears only under HKCU → confirms the per-user-visibility root cause.
187	- If the GDI family name differs from `Hack Nerd Font Mono` → instead fix the string.
188	
189	**Fix (recommended, if HKCU-only):** install Hack-NF-Mono **machine-wide** so Alacritty
190	sees it. Add an admin-gated step that copies the scoop-installed `.ttf`s
191	(`~/scoop/apps/Hack-NF-Mono/current/*.ttf`) into `%WINDIR%\Fonts` + registers them in
192	HKLM (or documents `scoop install -g Hack-NF-Mono`). Fits alongside the W5 admin-gated
193	script / `just` recipe pattern (e.g. a `just install-fonts-machine-wide` recipe), since
194	a non-elevated `chezmoi apply` can't write HKLM. Keep it fault-tolerant + opt-in.
195	
196	Files: (no config change) new admin step/recipe; docs note in `docs/tools.md`/`.zh-TW`.

200	## Workstream 7 — WezTerm config (opens cmd instead of pwsh)  [NEW]
201	
202	The repo installs WezTerm (`run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl:254`) as the
203	tmux-like multiplexer but ships **no config**, so it defaults to `cmd.exe`.
204	
205	Add a managed **`dot_config/wezterm/wezterm.lua`** → `~/.config/wezterm/wezterm.lua`
206	(WezTerm honors the XDG path on Windows; consistent with starship/yazi placement).
207	Minimal, mirroring the Alacritty/WT choices:
208	- `config.default_prog = { 'pwsh.exe', '-NoLogo' }` (pwsh resolves via scoop shims on PATH).

## Approved Plan (edited by user):
# Fresh-machine setup fixes + OpenSSH server

## Context

Setting up `hanruzhou`'s new (OneDrive-KFM, Microsoft-corp) Windows box surfaced
several defects and one feature request. All are independent and should land as
**separate commits**, but are planned together because they came from one setup log.

1. **OneDrive `$PROFILE` redirect is fragile** (the core request). On a machine
   where `Documents` is OneDrive-redirected, `run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1`
   writes a *stub* at the real `$PROFILE` that dot-sources the **literal**
   `~/Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1` — which need not exist
   there under KFM. Result: `The term '...\Documents\PowerShell\...ps1' is not
   recognized`. The managed loader is location-independent (only references
   `~/.config/powershell`), so the stub-pointer indirection is unnecessary and
   should be replaced by writing the loader **content** directly.
2. **pwsh startup takes 8827 ms.** `10_tools.ps1` cold-spawns 5–6 init binaries on
   every shell and does 3 full `PSModulePath` scans (slow under OneDrive hydration).
3. **`uv python install --default` fails** on uv 0.11+ (`--default` moved behind
   `--preview`). Stale "needs uv >= 0.8" comment; command echoed in 4 docs files.
4. **msstore ChatGPT ID is wrong** — mislabeled, not just dead (see IDs below).
5. **New request: install/enable the OpenSSH server (sshd).**
6. **Alacritty can't find `Hack Nerd Font Mono`** (Windows Terminal can) — the font
   is registered per-user (HKCU) by scoop's nerd-fonts bucket, and Alacritty on
   Windows reads only machine-wide (HKLM) fonts.
7. **WezTerm opens `cmd.exe`, not pwsh** — the repo ships no WezTerm config at all,
   so `default_prog` falls back to DOS.

Verified facts from exploration:
- CI (`.github/workflows/windows.yml:100-113`) auto-discovers every `*.ps1.tmpl`
  via `Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Filter *.ps1.tmpl` and render+parse-checks it — a
  renamed/added `.tmpl` needs **no** manual CI list edit.
- `{{ include }}`-into-single-quoted-here-string pattern already exists in
  `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_20_editor_overlays.ps1.tmpl:10-15`.
- `run_onchange` re-fires when the **rendered** script content changes, so embedding
  the loader via `{{ include }}` makes the redirect re-run whenever the loader edits.
- `$env:XDG_CACHE_HOME` is set in `00_env.ps1:18` (runs before `10_tools.ps1`).
- Loader source is verbatim `Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1`
  (no `dot_`/`.tmpl`, not in `.chezmoiignore`, no `.chezmoiroot`).

---

## Workstream 1 — OneDrive `$PROFILE` redirect (core)

**Convert** `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1`
→ `run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1.tmpl` (git mv/rename).

Change the write logic (currently `.ps1:47-51`): when `$realFull -ine $managedFull`,
instead of writing a stub that dot-sources `$managedFull`, write the **full managed
loader content** to `$real`, with a marker header line so we can detect our own copy.

- Embed the loader once, mirroring `run_onchange_after_20_editor_overlays.ps1.tmpl:10-15`:
  ```powershell
  $loader = @'
  {{ include "Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1" }}
  '@
  ```
  Single-quoted here-string is required — the loader contains `$HOME`, `$env:...`,
  `$($fragment.Name)` that must stay literal.
- Written file = marker line + loader body, e.g. first line
  `# Managed by chezmoi (dotfiles-windows) — OneDrive KFM redirect copy. Overrides: ~/.config/powershell/local.ps1`.
- **Keep** the existing once-only backup to
  `~/.dotfiles-backup/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1.onedrive.bak` and the marker-based
  "is this already our copy?" skip (`.ps1:33-45`) — update the marker string used by both.
- Add an idempotency guard: if `$real` already equals the intended content, skip the
  write (avoid churn; run_onchange already gates cross-apply re-runs).
- No-redirect path (`$realFull -ieq $managedFull`) stays a no-op; chezmoi manages the
  literal file directly. Keep `$ErrorActionPreference='Continue'` + try/catch (never abort).

**Why content, not pointer:** removes all dependency on the literal `~/Documents`
copy existing; eliminates any self-source recursion risk; and (via `{{ include }}`
re-hashing) future loader edits now propagate to the OneDrive copy automatically —
a latent bug the current plain-`.ps1` stub has (it never updates on loader change).

Files: `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1` (→ `.tmpl`).

---

## Workstream 2 — pwsh startup performance

Primary lever: **cache each tool's init output** to `$XDG_CACHE_HOME/pwsh-init/`,
keyed on the tool exe's `LastWriteTimeUtc` (auto-busts on scoop upgrade), so warm
shells dot-source a static file instead of cold-spawning the binary.

In `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/10_tools.ps1`:
- Add a helper `Import-CachedInit -Name <n> -Exe <exe> -Generate { ... }` at the top:
  resolves the exe, compares its mtime to a saved stamp, regenerates
  `pwsh-init/<n>.ps1` on miss/staleness, then dot-sources it. Guard on
  `Get-Command $Exe`; wrap generation in try/catch (never error the prompt).
- Route the 5 spawns through it:
  - starship (`:5-7`), zoxide (`:10-12`), atuin (`:18-20`), direnv (`:35-37`),
    tv (`:43-47`, keeping the `power-shell`→`powershell` fallback inside the generator).
  These emit **static** init text (no per-session state), so caching is safe.
- Replace `Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name X` (full PSModulePath scan) with direct
  `Import-Module X -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue` + a cheap `Get-Module -Name X`
  (loaded-only) check, in three places: PSFzf (`10_tools.ps1:28-31`),
  Copilot (`40_copilot.ps1:4-8`), PSReadLine (`90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl:3-4`).

Minor / optional (call out, low priority):
- `98_tv_cache.ps1` regenerates `aliases.txt` every start — add a "skip if content
  unchanged" or mtime guard.
- The loader has no interactive gate; a non-interactive early-return is low value
  (chezmoi runs scripts with `-NoProfile`) — leave unless measurement says otherwise.

**Measure first:** 8827 ms is partly first-run cold cost (Defender scanning freshly
downloaded exes + OneDrive hydration). After a couple of warm `Measure-Command { pwsh
-NoLogo -Command exit }` runs, the steady-state number tells us how much caching buys.

Files: `10_tools.ps1`, `40_copilot.ps1`, `90_psreadline.ps1.tmpl`, `98_tv_cache.ps1`.

---

## Workstream 3 — `uv python install --default`

In `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl:190-192`: add `--preview`
(`uv python install --default --preview`) and update the stale comment at `:188`
("Needs uv >= 0.8" → the flag now requires `--preview` on uv 0.8+/0.11+).

Docs mirror (same commit): drop-in the `--preview` form in `docs/tools.md:38`,
`docs/rationale.md:55`, `docs/tools.zh-TW.md:37`, `docs/rationale.zh-TW.md:47-48`.

---

## Workstream 4 — msstore ChatGPT ID (verification-gated)

Confirmed via Store listings: `9NT1R1C2HH7J` = **"ChatGPT Classic"** (fails / not the
app we want), `9PLM9XGG6VKS` = the real OpenAI **"ChatGPT"** (installs). Our script
labels them backwards at `run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl:272-274`.

Recommended change (after a `winget search --source msstore ChatGPT` on the box to
confirm region availability): drop the dead `9NT1R1C2HH7J`, keep `9PLM9XGG6VKS`
relabeled `# ChatGPT (OpenAI)`. Codex CLI is already covered by npm `@openai/codex`
(coding-agents block), so no separate "Codex" Store app is needed — if one is wanted,
look up its live ID rather than reusing `9PLM9XGG6VKS`.

Docs mirror: `docs/tools.md:57-58` + `docs/tools.zh-TW.md:57-58`.

## Workstream 5 — OpenSSH server (sshd)  [NEW]

Add an **opt-in, admin-gated, off-by-default** feature to install + enable the
Microsoft OpenSSH Server.

⚠️ **Caveat to confirm:** this opens an inbound listener (TCP 22) and enables a
system service — on a **corporate/managed** machine this may violate security policy
or be blocked. Default the toggle **off**; do not force it on. (Open decision: whether
to also hard-skip when `managedMachine` is true, like Tailscale/Grammarly — leaning
*no*, so you can knowingly opt in on the corp box, but with a printed warning.)

- **New init prompt** in `.chezmoi.toml.tmpl` via `promptBoolOnce`, default `false`.
  Proposed exact text (no `=`, per invariant #1):
  `Install and enable the OpenSSH server / sshd (needs admin, opens inbound port 22)?`
- **New dedicated script** `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_40_openssh_server.ps1.tmpl`,
  gated `{{ if .installSshServer }}` (separate from the user-scoped package installer
  because it needs admin + touches services/firewall — mirrors the dedicated
  `run_onchange_after_30_windows_terminal.ps1`). Fault-tolerant (`Continue`, never abort):
  - If not elevated (reuse a `Test-Admin` like `bootstrap.ps1:36-42`): print guidance
    to run `just enable-sshd` from an elevated pwsh, then return.
  - `Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name OpenSSH.Server~~~~0.0.1.0`
  - `Set-Service sshd -StartupType Automatic; Start-Service sshd`
  - Ensure firewall rule `OpenSSH-Server-In-TCP` (create if missing).
  - Optional: set pwsh as sshd `DefaultShell` (HKLM; admin).
- **`just enable-sshd` recipe** in `justfile` as the elevated fallback (mirrors
  `just upgrade-scoop`), running the same commands.
- **Invariant #1 (same commit):** add `--promptBool "<exact prompt text>=false"` to
  `.github/workflows/windows.yml` init flags, matching the prompt text verbatim.

---

## Workstream 6 — Alacritty Nerd Font (verification-gated)

Root cause (to confirm on the box): Alacritty on Windows enumerates only the
**machine-wide** font collection, so a Nerd Font registered **per-user** by scoop is
invisible to it — while Windows Terminal (which resolves the *same* `Hack Nerd Font
Mono` string, `run_onchange_after_30_windows_terminal.ps1:36`) and WezTerm both see
HKCU fonts. The Alacritty config family string (`alacritty.toml.tmpl:18-21`) is
correct and needs **no** change.

**Diagnose first (Windows box):**
```powershell
[System.Drawing.FontFamily]::Families.Name | Select-String Hack   # exact family name Alacritty would match
Get-ItemProperty 'HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Fonts' | Format-List *Hack*
Get-ItemProperty 'HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Fonts' | Format-List *Hack*
```
- If `Hack*` appears only under HKCU → confirms the per-user-visibility root cause.
- If the GDI family name differs from `Hack Nerd Font Mono` → instead fix the string.

**Fix (recommended, if HKCU-only):** install Hack-NF-Mono **machine-wide** so Alacritty
sees it. Add an admin-gated step that copies the scoop-installed `.ttf`s
(`~/scoop/apps/Hack-NF-Mono/current/*.ttf`) into `%WINDIR%\Fonts` + registers them in
HKLM (or documents `scoop install -g Hack-NF-Mono`). Fits alongside the W5 admin-gated
script / `just` recipe pattern (e.g. a `just install-fonts-machine-wide` recipe), since
a non-elevated `chezmoi apply` can't write HKLM. Keep it fault-tolerant + opt-in.

Files: (no config change) new admin step/recipe; docs note in `docs/tools.md`/`.zh-TW`.

---

## Workstream 7 — WezTerm config (opens cmd instead of pwsh)  [NEW]

The repo installs WezTerm (`run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl:254`) as the
tmux-like multiplexer but ships **no config**, so it defaults to `cmd.exe`.

Add a managed **`dot_config/wezterm/wezterm.lua`** → `~/.config/wezterm/wezterm.lua`
(WezTerm honors the XDG path on Windows; consistent with starship/yazi placement).
Minimal, mirroring the Alacritty/WT choices:
- `config.default_prog = { 'pwsh.exe', '-NoLogo' }` (pwsh resolves via scoop shims on PATH).
- `config.font = wezterm.font('Hack Nerd Font Mono')` — note WezTerm sees the per-user
  font (unlike Alacritty), so no machine-wide dependency here.
- font size, color scheme (e.g. `One Half Dark` to match WT), tab-bar basics.

Plain `.lua` (no templating needed). New config surface → mention in `docs/tools.md` +
`.zh-TW`, and optionally the skill "Config surfaces" note. Fold WezTerm into the
"editors/terminals" mental model in `AGENTS.md`/`CLAUDE.md` architecture bullet if desired.

Files: new `dot_config/wezterm/wezterm.lua`; docs mirror.

87	          Write-Host '--- deployed tree ---'

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-10 17:05:40Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tmpl
#Requires -Version 7
# run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1.tmpl — make sure pwsh actually loads
# the managed $PROFILE, even when OneDrive redirects Documents.
#
# chezmoi writes the loader to the LITERAL ~/Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.
# PowerShell_profile.ps1. But when OneDrive "Known Folder Move" is enabled (very
# common on Windows, and mandatory on corp-managed boxes), pwsh resolves $PROFILE
# under "~/OneDrive - <tenant>/Documents/PowerShell/..." instead — so the managed
# profile silently never loads: no starship, no aliases, just the bare prompt.
#
# We can't know that redirected path at apply time, but THIS script runs under
# pwsh, so $PROFILE here is exactly what an interactive pwsh computes. If it
# differs from the managed file, we write the managed loader's CONTENT there
# directly. Pure no-op when Documents isn't redirected.
#
# Why content, not a `. "<literal path>"` stub (the previous approach): under KFM
# the literal ~/Documents copy is NOT guaranteed to exist at $PROFILE-load time,
# so a stub pointing at it fails with
#   The term '...\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1' is not recognized
# The loader is location-independent (it only sources ~/.config/powershell), so
# embedding its body is strictly more robust. Embedding via {{ "{{" }} include {{ "}}" }}
# also folds the loader's bytes into this script's rendered content, so
# run_onchange re-fires whenever the loader changes and the OneDrive copy stays in
# sync. See pitfalls/onedrive-kfm-profile-not-loaded.md.
#
# Fault-tolerant per the repo invariant: never abort the apply.
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'

# Managed loader body, embedded verbatim at render time. Single-quoted here-string
# keeps $HOME / $env: / $($fragment.Name) literal. Mirrors the include pattern in
# run_onchange_after_20_editor_overlays.ps1.tmpl.
$loaderBody = @'
{{ include "Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1" }}
'@

# ASCII-only marker (bulletproof for the read-back equality check below).
$marker  = '# Managed by chezmoi (dotfiles-windows) - OneDrive KFM redirect copy. Local overrides: ~/.config/powershell/local.ps1'
$content = $marker + "`n" + $loaderBody
$ourMark = 'chezmoi (dotfiles-windows)'   # matches this copy AND the old stub

$managed = Join-Path $HOME 'Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1'
$real    = $PROFILE   # CurrentUserCurrentHost — OneDrive-aware

try {
    $managedFull = [System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath($managed)
    $realFull    = if ($real) { [System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath([string]$real) } else { '' }

    if (-not $realFull -or ($realFull -ieq $managedFull)) {
        Write-Host '==> $PROFILE points at the managed loader (no redirect needed)' -ForegroundColor DarkGray
        return
    }

    $dir = Split-Path $realFull -Parent
    if (-not (Test-Path $dir)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $dir | Out-Null }

    if (Test-Path -LiteralPath $realFull) {
        $existing = Get-Content -LiteralPath $realFull -Raw -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

        # Already our managed copy with identical content — nothing to do.
        if ($existing -eq $content) {
            Write-Host '==> OneDrive $PROFILE already up to date (no change)' -ForegroundColor DarkGray
            return
        }

        # Preserve a PRE-EXISTING user profile before we overwrite it — the
        # run_once_before backup only guards the literal ~/Documents path, not
        # this OneDrive-redirected one. Skip if it's already ours (managed copy
        # or the old redirect stub); back up once otherwise.
        if ($existing -and ($existing -notmatch [regex]::Escape($ourMark))) {
            $backupDir = Join-Path $HOME '.dotfiles-backup'
            New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $backupDir | Out-Null
            $bak = Join-Path $backupDir 'Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1.onedrive.bak'
            if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $bak)) {
                Copy-Item -LiteralPath $realFull -Destination $bak -Force
                Write-Host "==> backed up existing profile -> $bak" -ForegroundColor Cyan
            }
        }
    }

    Set-Content -LiteralPath $realFull -Value $content -Encoding utf8
    Write-Host "==> Wrote managed profile to OneDrive `$PROFILE -> $realFull" -ForegroundColor Cyan
} catch {
    Write-Warning "profile redirect failed (non-fatal): $_"
}

```

1	#Requires -Version 7
2	# run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1 — make sure pwsh actually loads the
3	# managed $PROFILE.
4	#
5	# chezmoi writes the loader to the LITERAL ~/Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-10 17:06:11Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tmpl
#Requires -Version 7
# run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1.tmpl — make sure pwsh actually loads
# the managed $PROFILE, even when OneDrive redirects Documents.
#
# chezmoi writes the loader to the LITERAL ~/Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.
# PowerShell_profile.ps1. But when OneDrive "Known Folder Move" is enabled (very
# common on Windows, and mandatory on corp-managed boxes), pwsh resolves $PROFILE
# under "~/OneDrive - <tenant>/Documents/PowerShell/..." instead — so the managed
# profile silently never loads: no starship, no aliases, just the bare prompt.
#
# We can't know that redirected path at apply time, but THIS script runs under
# pwsh, so $PROFILE here is exactly what an interactive pwsh computes. If it
# differs from the managed file, we write the managed loader's CONTENT there
# directly. Pure no-op when Documents isn't redirected.
#
# Why content, not a `. "<literal path>"` stub (the previous approach): under KFM
# the literal ~/Documents copy is NOT guaranteed to exist at $PROFILE-load time,
# so a stub pointing at it fails with
#   The term '...\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1' is not recognized
# The loader is location-independent (it only sources ~/.config/powershell), so
# embedding its body is strictly more robust. Embedding via {{ "{{" }} include {{ "}}" }}
# also folds the loader's bytes into this script's rendered content, so
# run_onchange re-fires whenever the loader changes and the OneDrive copy stays in
# sync. See pitfalls/onedrive-kfm-profile-not-loaded.md.
#
# Fault-tolerant per the repo invariant: never abort the apply.
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'

# Managed loader body, embedded verbatim at render time. Single-quoted here-string
# keeps $HOME / $env: / $($fragment.Name) literal. Mirrors the include pattern in
# run_onchange_after_20_editor_overlays.ps1.tmpl.
$loaderBody = @'
{{ include "Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1" }}
'@

# ASCII-only marker (bulletproof for the read-back equality check below).
$marker  = '# Managed by chezmoi (dotfiles-windows) - OneDrive KFM redirect copy. Local overrides: ~/.config/powershell/local.ps1'
$content = $marker + "`n" + $loaderBody
$ourMark = 'chezmoi (dotfiles-windows)'   # matches this copy AND the old redirect stub

$managed = Join-Path $HOME 'Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1'
$real    = $PROFILE   # CurrentUserCurrentHost — OneDrive-aware

try {
    $managedFull = [System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath($managed)
    $realFull    = if ($real) { [System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath([string]$real) } else { '' }

    if (-not $realFull -or ($realFull -ieq $managedFull)) {
        Write-Host '==> $PROFILE points at the managed loader (no redirect needed)' -ForegroundColor DarkGray
        return
    }

    $dir = Split-Path $realFull -Parent
    if (-not (Test-Path $dir)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $dir | Out-Null }

    if (Test-Path -LiteralPath $realFull) {
        $existing = Get-Content -LiteralPath $realFull -Raw -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

        # Already our managed copy with identical content — nothing to do.
        if ($existing -eq $content) {
            Write-Host '==> OneDrive $PROFILE already up to date (no change)' -ForegroundColor DarkGray
            return
        }

        # Preserve a PRE-EXISTING user profile before we overwrite it — the
        # run_once_before backup only guards the literal ~/Documents path, not
        # this OneDrive-redirected one. Skip if it's already ours (managed copy
        # or the old redirect stub); back up once otherwise.
        if ($existing -and ($existing -notmatch [regex]::Escape($ourMark))) {
            $backupDir = Join-Path $HOME '.dotfiles-backup'
            New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $backupDir | Out-Null
            $bak = Join-Path $backupDir 'Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1.onedrive.bak'
            if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $bak)) {
                Copy-Item -LiteralPath $realFull -Destination $bak -Force
                Write-Host "==> backed up existing profile -> $bak" -ForegroundColor Cyan
            }
        }
    }

    Set-Content -LiteralPath $realFull -Value $content -Encoding utf8
    Write-Host "==> Wrote managed profile to OneDrive `$PROFILE -> $realFull" -ForegroundColor Cyan
} catch {
    Write-Warning "profile redirect failed (non-fatal): $_"
}

1	#Requires -Version 7
2	# run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1 — install the toolset with scoop (CLI) +
3	# winget (GUI). Idempotent: only missing packages are installed. This script is
4	# the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for what gets installed; re-runs whenever the
5	# rendered content (i.e. any toggle below) changes.
6	#
7	# Toggles (from .chezmoi.toml.tmpl):
8	#   installCodingAgents  = {{ .installCodingAgents }}
9	#   installWindowsApps   = {{ .installWindowsApps }}
10	#   installUtilityApps   = {{ .installUtilityApps }}
11	#   installGamingApps    = {{ .installGamingApps }}
12	#   installExtraRuntimes = {{ .installExtraRuntimes }}
13	#   installMediaTools    = {{ .installMediaTools }}
14	#   installLlmTools      = {{ .installLlmTools }}
15	#   installTunnelTools   = {{ .installTunnelTools }}
16	#   installIacTools      = {{ .installIacTools }}
17	#   useChineseMirror     = {{ .useChineseMirror }}
18	#   managedMachine       = {{ .managedMachine }}
19	
20	# Resilient by design: individual package failures are collected and reported at
21	# the end, never fatal — one bad manifest must not abort the rest of the install
22	# (nor the rest of `chezmoi apply`). Mirrors ansible allow-partial-failure.
23	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'
24	if (Test-Path variable:PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference) { $PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference = $false }
25	$script:Failures = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]::new()
26	
27	# UTF-8 console so winget's localized output isn't mojibake on zh-* Windows.
28	try { [Console]::OutputEncoding = [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false) } catch { $null = $_ }
29	{{ if .useChineseMirror }}
30	# China (GFW) mirrors for install-time package fetches.
31	$env:PIP_INDEX_URL = 'https://pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/simple'
32	$env:UV_DEFAULT_INDEX = 'https://pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/simple'
33	$env:npm_config_registry = 'https://registry.npmmirror.com'
34	$env:GOPROXY = 'https://goproxy.cn,direct'
35	$env:MISE_NODE_MIRROR_URL = 'https://npmmirror.com/mirrors/node/'
36	{{ end }}
37	
38	function Info($m) { Write-Host "==> $m" -ForegroundColor Cyan }
39	function Have($n) { [bool](Get-Command $n -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) }
40	function Register-Failure($what) { $script:Failures.Add($what); Write-Warning "FAILED (skipped): $what" }
41	
42	# --- scoop -----------------------------------------------------------------
43	function Test-Admin {
44	    # $true when this pwsh is running elevated (member of the Administrators role).
45	    try {
46	        $id = [Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent()
47	        ([Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal]$id).IsInRole([Security.Principal.WindowsBuiltInRole]::Administrator)
48	    } catch { $false }
49	}
50	
51	function Ensure-Scoop {
52	    if (-not (Have scoop)) {
53	        # get.scoop.sh returns install.ps1's text. Piping it to Invoke-Expression
54	        # works in a normal console, but an ELEVATED one is refused ("Running the
55	        # installer as administrator is disabled by default") unless -RunAsAdmin is
56	        # passed. Some dev boxes always open the shell as admin, so detect that and
57	        # forward the flag. We build a scriptblock from the fetched text so we can
58	        # pass args -- equivalent to `iex "& {$(irm get.scoop.sh)} -RunAsAdmin"`.
59	        $installer = [scriptblock]::Create((Invoke-RestMethod get.scoop.sh))
60	        if (Test-Admin) {
61	            Info 'scoop not found — installing (elevated console: passing -RunAsAdmin)'
62	            & $installer -RunAsAdmin
63	        } else {
64	            Info 'scoop not found — installing (user-scoped, no admin)'
65	            & $installer
66	        }
67	    }
68	    foreach ($b in 'main', 'extras', 'versions', 'nerd-fonts') {
69	        scoop bucket add $b *> $null
70	    }
71	}
72	
73	function Get-ScoopInstalled {
74	    try { return @((scoop export | ConvertFrom-Json).apps.name) } catch { return @() }
75	}
76	
77	function Scoop-Install([string[]]$apps) {
78	    if (-not $apps) { return }
79	    # Wrap in @() so a lone survivor stays an array. Without it PowerShell
80	    # unwraps a single match to a bare string, and `scoop install @missing`
81	    # then splats it one CHARACTER per arg => "Couldn't find manifest for 'm'"
82	    # when only "mise" was missing.
83	    $missing = @($apps | Where-Object { ($_ -split '/')[-1] -notin (Get-ScoopInstalled) })
84	    if (-not $missing) { Info 'scoop: all requested tools already present'; return }
85	    Info "scoop install: $($missing -join ', ')"
86	    try { scoop install @missing } catch { Write-Warning "scoop batch error: $_" }
87	    # Anything still absent after the batch = failed (scoop keeps going past a bad one).
88	    $after = Get-ScoopInstalled
89	    foreach ($app in $missing) {
90	        $name = ($app -split '/')[-1]
91	        if ($name -notin $after) { Register-Failure "scoop:$name" }
92	    }
93	}
94	
95	# --- winget ----------------------------------------------------------------
96	function Winget-Install([string]$id) {
97	    if (-not (Have winget)) { Register-Failure "winget:$id (winget unavailable)"; return }
98	    winget list --id $id -e --accept-source-agreements *> $null
99	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { return }   # already installed
100	    Info "winget install $id"
101	    winget install --id $id -e --silent --scope user `
102	        --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
103	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
104	        # some packages don't support --scope user; retry machine scope.
105	        winget install --id $id -e --silent `
106	            --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
107	    }
108	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "winget:$id" }
109	}
110	
111	# Microsoft Store packages (installed by Store product id via the msstore
112	# source) — used for apps with no community winget manifest, e.g. the official
113	# ChatGPT / Codex desktop apps. msstore ignores --scope, so we don't pass it.
114	function Winget-InstallStore([string]$id) {
115	    if (-not (Have winget)) { Register-Failure "msstore:$id (winget unavailable)"; return }
116	    winget list --id $id -e --accept-source-agreements *> $null
117	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { return }   # already installed
118	    Info "winget (msstore) install $id"
119	    winget install --id $id -e --source msstore --silent `
120	        --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
121	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "msstore:$id" }
122	}
123	
124	# --- npm CLI agents --------------------------------------------------------
125	function Npm-InstallGlobal([string[]]$pkgs) {
126	    if (-not (Have npm)) { foreach ($p in $pkgs) { Register-Failure "npm:$p (npm unavailable)" }; return }
127	    foreach ($p in $pkgs) {
128	        Info "npm i -g $p"
129	        npm install -g $p 2>&1 | Out-Host
130	        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "npm:$p" }
131	    }
132	}
133	
134	# ===========================================================================
135	Ensure-Scoop
136	
137	# Core CLI toolset — the shell baseline, always installed.
138	Scoop-Install @(
139	    'git', 'gh', 'glab', '7zip', 'make', 'just',
140	    'starship', 'zoxide', 'fzf', 'fd', 'ripgrep', 'bat', 'eza', 'jq',
141	    'delta', 'lazygit', 'neovim', 'yazi', 'btop', 'television', 'tlrc',
142	    'uv', 'gnupg',
143	    # Runtimes are NATIVE via scoop, not mise: mise's activate/shims are
144	    # unreliable on Windows and it has no windows-arm64 bun build. node + bun
145	    # here; Python via uv (below); go/rust/ruby under Extra runtimes.
146	    # copilot-proxy needs bun/bunx.
147	    'nodejs-lts', 'bun',
148	    # LazyVim needs a C compiler for tree-sitter parsers (zig), the tree-sitter
149	    # CLI the nvim-treesitter `main` branch requires, and a clipboard provider so
150	    # `unnamedplus` yanks reach the Windows clipboard (win32yank).
151	    'zig', 'tree-sitter', 'win32yank'
152	)
153	
154	# On Windows-on-ARM, scoop's neovim is amd64 (runs under x64 emulation) while
155	# zig installs arm64-native, so zig-built tree-sitter parsers are the wrong
156	# arch for nvim. Add an amd64 gcc that matches the emulated nvim.
157	if ([System.Runtime.InteropServices.RuntimeInformation]::OSArchitecture -eq 'Arm64') {
158	    Info 'ARM64 host: adding amd64 gcc for nvim-treesitter'
159	    Scoop-Install @('gcc')
160	}
161	
162	# gh-dash: GitHub PR/issue dashboard TUI (`gh dash`). It's a gh extension, not a
163	# scoop package, so it needs gh to be authenticated -- this no-ops on a fresh
164	# machine until `gh auth login`. run_onchange only re-fires when this script
165	# changes, so after authenticating, install by hand (`gh extension install
166	# dlvhdr/gh-dash`) or force a reapply (`chezmoi state delete-bucket
167	# --bucket=entryState`). gh-notify / gh-select from the Unix repo are bash-only
168	# extensions and are intentionally skipped on Windows.
169	if (Have gh) {
170	    gh auth status *> $null
171	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
172	        $ghExts = gh extension list 2>$null | Out-String
173	        if ($ghExts -notmatch 'dlvhdr/gh-dash') {
174	            Info 'gh extension install dlvhdr/gh-dash'
175	            gh extension install dlvhdr/gh-dash
176	            if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'gh-ext:gh-dash' }
177	        }
178	    } else {
179	        Info 'gh not authenticated -- skipping gh-dash (gh auth login, then reapply)'
180	    }
181	}
182	
183	# node/bun come from scoop above (on ~/scoop/shims, reliably on PATH). We do NOT
184	# use mise here -- see the runtimes note in the core scoop list.
185	
186	# Default Python, uv-managed. --default also creates unversioned python/python3
187	# (not just python3.x) in UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR (~/.local/bin, on PATH via 00_env
188	# ahead of the Store's python.exe app-alias). Needs uv >= 0.8.
189	if (Have uv) {
190	    Info 'uv python install --default'
191	    $env:UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR = Join-Path $HOME '.local\bin'
192	    uv python install --default 2>&1 | Out-Host
193	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'uv:python' }
194	}
195	
196	# Make PSGallery usable non-interactively before Install-Module (NuGet provider
197	# + trust) so the fzf key bindings / audio cmdlets install reliably.
198	try {
199	    if (-not (Get-PackageProvider -Name NuGet -ListAvailable -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) {
200	        Install-PackageProvider -Name NuGet -Scope CurrentUser -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Out-Null
201	    }
202	    if ((Get-PSRepository -Name PSGallery -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).InstallationPolicy -ne 'Trusted') {
203	        Set-PSRepository -Name PSGallery -InstallationPolicy Trusted -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
204	    }
205	} catch { Write-Warning "PSGallery prep failed (non-fatal): $_" }
206	
207	# PSReadLine ships with pwsh; PSFzf powers the fzf key bindings in profile.d
208	# (Ctrl+t = files, Ctrl+r = fuzzy history search).
209	if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name PSFzf)) {
210	    Info 'Install-Module PSFzf'
211	    Install-Module PSFzf -Scope CurrentUser -Force -AcceptLicense -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
212	}
213	# AudioDeviceCmdlets: absolute volume + explicit mute for the sys* helpers.
214	if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name AudioDeviceCmdlets)) {
215	    Info 'Install-Module AudioDeviceCmdlets'
216	    Install-Module AudioDeviceCmdlets -Scope CurrentUser -Force -AcceptLicense -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
217	}
218	
219	{{ if .installExtraRuntimes -}}
220	# Extra language runtimes, native via scoop (rust via rustup). node/bun/uv are
221	# baseline; go/rust/ruby added here.
222	Scoop-Install @('go', 'rustup', 'ruby')
223	{{ end -}}
224	
225	{{ if .installMediaTools -}}
226	Scoop-Install @('ffmpeg', 'imagemagick')
227	{{ end -}}
228	
229	{{ if .installCodingAgents -}}
230	# AI coding agents (npm-based CLIs; Antigravity IDE goes via winget below).
231	Npm-InstallGlobal @(
232	    '@anthropic-ai/claude-code',
233	    'opencode-ai',
234	    '@openai/codex',
235	    '@github/copilot'
236	)
237	# NOTE: SpecStory ships no native-Windows CLI (macOS: brew; Linux/WSL: GitHub
238	# release binaries only), so it is intentionally omitted -- `npm i -g
239	# @specstory/cli` 404s. The Copilot module already no-ops when `specstory` is
240	# absent. Experimental Windows build from the unmerged PR #191:
241	# `just specstory-build` (see backlog/specstory-windows-native-cli.md).
242	
243	# OpenCode Desktop (GUI companion to the opencode-ai CLI) via scoop extras.
244	Scoop-Install @('extras/opencode-desktop')
245	{{ end -}}
246	
247	{{ if .installWindowsApps -}}
248	# GUI apps + editors via winget.
249	foreach ($id in @(
250	    'Microsoft.VisualStudioCode',
251	    'Anysphere.Cursor',
252	    'Microsoft.WindowsTerminal',
253	    'Alacritty.Alacritty',
254	    'wez.wezterm',
255	    'Microsoft.PowerToys',
256	    'Google.Antigravity',
257	    'Docker.DockerDesktop',
258	    'Discord.Discord',
259	    # AI desktop app + browsers.
260	    'Anthropic.Claude',
261	    'Google.Chrome',
262	    'TheBrowserCompany.Arc',
263	    'Zen-Team.Zen-Browser'
264	{{- if not .managedMachine }},
265	    'Grammarly.Grammarly'   # skipped on managed machines (often policy-blocked)
266	{{- end }}
267	)) { Winget-Install $id }
268	
269	# Store-only apps: Raycast (default launcher) + the official ChatGPT / Codex
270	# desktop apps. All ship via the Microsoft Store; there's no Raycast.Raycast
271	# winget manifest, so it installs by Store product id.
272	Winget-InstallStore '9PFXXSHC64H3'   # Raycast
273	Winget-InstallStore '9NT1R1C2HH7J'   # ChatGPT
274	Winget-InstallStore '9PLM9XGG6VKS'   # Codex
275	
276	# Nerd Fonts so starship glyphs render. Hack-NF-Mono supplies the
277	# "Hack Nerd Font Mono" family the Alacritty / Windows Terminal / editor
278	# configs reference; Hack-NF ("Hack Nerd Font") is the proportional companion.
279	Scoop-Install @('Hack-NF', 'Hack-NF-Mono')
280	
281	Write-Warning 'Raycast is the default launcher but shares Alt+Space with PowerToys Run -- disable PowerToys Run (PowerToys Settings > PowerToys Run > off) or rebind a hotkey. Raycast for Windows is a Store beta; if it fails to install, Flow Launcher is the fallback: winget install Flow-Launcher.Flow-Launcher.'
282	{{ end -}}
283	
284	{{ if .installGamingApps -}}
285	Winget-Install 'Valve.Steam'
286	{{ end -}}
287	
288	{{ if .installUtilityApps -}}
289	# System / utility apps via winget.
290	foreach ($id in @(
291	    'CPUID.CPU-Z',
292	    'TechPowerUp.GPU-Z',
293	    'REALiX.HWiNFO',
294	    'JAMSoftware.TreeSize.Free',
295	    'WinDirStat.WinDirStat',
296	    'voidtools.Everything',
297	    'ShareX.ShareX',
298	    'VideoLAN.VLC',
299	    'OBSProject.OBSStudio',
300	    'Rufus.Rufus'
301	{{- if not .managedMachine }},
302	    'Tailscale.Tailscale'   # skipped on managed machines (org policy blocks the MSI, exit 1625)
303	{{- end }}
304	)) { Winget-Install $id }
305	{{ end -}}
306	
307	{{ if .installLlmTools -}}
308	# Local LLM tools: Ollama (winget) + LiteLLM (uv tool).
309	Winget-Install 'Ollama.Ollama'
310	if (Have uv) { Info 'uv tool install litellm'; uv tool install 'litellm[proxy]'; if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'uv:litellm' } }
311	{{ end -}}
312	
313	{{ if .installTunnelTools -}}

315	{{ end -}}
316

319	Winget-Install 'Microsoft.AzureCLI'
320	# Azure DevOps CLI (az extension -> `az devops`, `az repos`, `az pipelines`).
321	# It also auto-installs on first `az devops`/`az repos` use; pre-adding here just
322	# skips the first-use prompt. Skipped when az isn't on PATH yet (a fresh winget
323	# install needs a new shell) -- harmless, since first use self-installs it.
324	if (Have az) {
325	    Info 'az extension add azure-devops'
326	    az extension add --name azure-devops --upgrade --only-show-errors
327	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'az-ext:azure-devops' }
328	}
329	{{ end -}}
330	
331	if ($script:Failures.Count -gt 0) {
332	    Write-Warning ("package install: {0} item(s) failed and were skipped:" -f $script:Failures.Count)
333	    $script:Failures | ForEach-Object { Write-Warning "  - $_" }
334	    Write-Warning "Re-run 'just apply' (or 'chezmoi apply') to retry, or install them manually."
335	} else {
336	    Info 'package install complete — no failures'
337	}
338

2	# channel. tv runs channel commands in a fresh shell without our $PROFILE, so we

7	    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $dir | Out-Null
8	    $lines = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]::new()
9	    Get-Alias | Sort-Object Name | ForEach-Object { $lines.Add("$($_.Name) -> $($_.Definition)") }
10	    Get-Command -CommandType Function -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
11	        Where-Object { -not $_.Source -or $_.Source -eq 'Copilot' } |
12	        Sort-Object Name | ForEach-Object { $lines.Add("$($_.Name) (function)") }
13	    $lines | Set-Content -Path (Join-Path $dir 'aliases.txt') -Encoding utf8
14	}

<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```ps1
# 10_tools.ps1 — activate CLI tools. Each block no-ops if the tool is absent,
# so a partial install (or the minimal role) never errors the prompt.
#
# Tool `init` output (starship/zoxide/atuin/direnv/tv) is cached under
# $XDG_CACHE_HOME/pwsh-init so a warm shell dot-sources a static file instead of
# cold-spawning the binary on every start — the dominant pwsh-startup cost on a
# fresh box (each spawn is a process launch + Defender scan). The cache
# auto-regenerates when the tool's exe timestamp changes (e.g. a scoop upgrade);
# delete ~/.cache/pwsh-init to force a rebuild.
function Import-CachedInit {
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Name,
        [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Exe,
        [Parameter(Mandatory)][scriptblock]$Generate
    )
    $cmd = Get-Command $Exe -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    if (-not $cmd) { return }
    $cacheDir  = Join-Path $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME 'pwsh-init'
    $cacheFile = Join-Path $cacheDir "$Name.ps1"
    $stampFile = "$cacheFile.stamp"
    try { $stamp = (Get-Item -LiteralPath $cmd.Source).LastWriteTimeUtc.Ticks.ToString() } catch { $stamp = '' }
    $fresh = (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cacheFile) -and (Test-Path -LiteralPath $stampFile) -and
             ((Get-Content -LiteralPath $stampFile -Raw -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Trim() -eq $stamp)
    if (-not $fresh) {
        try {
            $out = & $Generate | Out-String
            if (-not $out.Trim()) { return }   # tool errored / empty output — retry next start
            if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cacheDir)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $cacheDir | Out-Null }
            Set-Content -LiteralPath $cacheFile -Value $out   -Encoding utf8
            Set-Content -LiteralPath $stampFile -Value $stamp -Encoding utf8
        } catch { return }
    }
    try { . $cacheFile } catch { Write-Warning "profile: cached init '$Name' failed: $_" }
}

# starship prompt
Import-CachedInit -Name 'starship' -Exe 'starship' -Generate { starship init powershell }

# zoxide — smarter cd (rebinds `cd` to `z`)
Import-CachedInit -Name 'zoxide' -Exe 'zoxide' -Generate { zoxide init powershell --cmd cd }

# Runtimes (node/bun/go/rust/ruby) are native via scoop and Python via uv — no
# mise on Windows (its activate/shims are unreliable here). See docs/rationale.

# atuin — SQLite shell history with fuzzy search (Ctrl+R)
Import-CachedInit -Name 'atuin' -Exe 'atuin' -Generate { atuin init powershell }

# fzf — fuzzy finder + PSFzf key bindings (Ctrl+t files, Ctrl+r history)
if (Get-Command fzf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
    $env:FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS = '--height 40% --layout=reverse --border'
    if (Get-Command fd -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
        $env:FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND = 'fd --hidden --strip-cwd-prefix --exclude .git'
    }
    # Import directly — a `Get-Module -ListAvailable` guard scans all of
    # PSModulePath (slow, esp. under OneDrive-hydrated Documents\PowerShell\Modules).
    # `Get-Module -Name` checks only loaded modules, so it's cheap.
    Import-Module PSFzf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    if (Get-Module -Name PSFzf) {
        Set-PsFzfOption -PSReadlineChordProvider 'Ctrl+t' -PSReadlineChordReverseHistory 'Ctrl+r'
    }
}

# direnv — per-directory environments
Import-CachedInit -Name 'direnv' -Exe 'direnv' -Generate { direnv hook pwsh }

# television (tv) — fuzzy picker shell integration. tv 0.15+ renamed the shell
# value `powershell` -> `power-shell`; older builds used `powershell`. Try the
# new spelling first, fall back, and only cache non-empty output so a version
# mismatch never errors the prompt.
Import-CachedInit -Name 'tv' -Exe 'tv' -Generate {
    $o = tv init power-shell 2>$null | Out-String
    if (-not $o.Trim()) { $o = tv init powershell 2>$null | Out-String }
    $o
}

```

 `./docs/tools.md`

```text
1	# Tools
2	
3	The install set lives in `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl`
4	(the single source of truth), gated by the init toggles. **scoop** handles CLI
5	tools; **winget** handles GUI apps.
6	
7	## CLI tools (scoop)
8	
9	| Tool | Purpose |
10	|---|---|
11	| git, gh, glab | version control + GitHub / GitLab CLIs |
12	| neovim | editor (LazyVim, pre-configured) |
13	| lazygit | git TUI (delta pager configured) |
14	| zoxide | smarter `cd` |
15	| fzf, fd, ripgrep | fuzzy find / file search / grep |
16	| bat, eza | `cat` / `ls` replacements |
17	| delta | git diff pager |
18	| jq | JSON processor |
19	| yazi, btop | file manager / system monitor |
20	| television (`tv`) | fuzzy picker / channel launcher |
21	| tldr (tlrc) | community man-page cheatsheets; `tldrf` adds `zh_TW → zh → en` fallback |
22	| gh dash | GitHub PR/issue dashboard TUI (gh extension; installs once `gh` is authenticated) |
23	| starship | prompt |
24	| node | JS runtime (`nodejs-lts`) |
25	| uv | Python package/runtime manager |
26	| bun | JS runtime + package manager (backs copilot-proxy) |
27	| just | task runner (this repo's `justfile`) |
28	| make | GNU make |
29	| zig | C compiler for Neovim tree-sitter parsers |
30	| gcc | amd64 C compiler (**ARM64 hosts only** — matches the x64-emulated nvim) |
31	| tree-sitter | tree-sitter CLI (nvim-treesitter `main` branch) |
32	| win32yank | clipboard provider for Neovim |
33	| gnupg | `gpg` (git commit signing, verification) |
34	
35	Runtimes are **native (scoop), not mise** — see [rationale](rationale.md#runtimes-scoop-natives-not-mise-on-windows).
36	`node` (`nodejs-lts`) and `bun` come from scoop; `go`, `rust` (rustup), `ruby`
37	are added when **Extra runtimes** is enabled. A default **Python** is uv-managed:
38	`uv python install --default` puts `python`/`python3` in `~/.local/bin` (on PATH
39	ahead of the Store's `python.exe` app-alias), so `python`, `uv run`, and
40	`uv venv` all work without the Microsoft Store.
41	
42	## GUI apps (winget)
43	
44	| App | winget id |
45	|---|---|

65	## Utility apps (winget)
66	
67	Enabled by **Install utility apps**:
68	
69	| App | winget id | What |
70	|---|---|---|
71	| CPU-Z | `CPUID.CPU-Z` | CPU/mainboard info |
72	| GPU-Z | `TechPowerUp.GPU-Z` | GPU info + sensors |

83	!!! note "Launchers & the Alt+Space clash"
84	    **Raycast** (Microsoft Store, id `9PFXXSHC64H3`) is the default launcher.
85	    It shares **Alt+Space** with **PowerToys Run** — disable PowerToys Run
86	    (PowerToys Settings › PowerToys Run › off) or rebind one. Raycast for Windows
87	    is still a Store beta; if it won't install, fall back to
88	    [Flow Launcher](https://www.flowlauncher.com/):
89	    `winget install Flow-Launcher.Flow-Launcher`.
90	
91	!!! note "Notepad++ is opt-in"
92	    Dropped from the default set: on Windows-on-ARM its installer is a
93	    low-reputation binary that Windows Defender / SmartScreen flags as PUA
94	    (a false positive, aggravated by managed-machine PUA protection), and it's
95	    redundant with VSCode / Cursor / nvim. Add it yourself if you want it:
96	    `winget install Notepad++.Notepad++` or `scoop install notepadplusplus`.
97	
98	!!! note "Managed / corporate machines"
99	    The **Managed machine** init toggle skips apps that org policy commonly
100	    blocks — **Grammarly** and **Tailscale** (its MSI fails with exit `1625`
101	    under policy). Leave the toggle off on a personal machine to install them.
102	
103	## AI agents (npm)
104	
105	`@anthropic-ai/claude-code`, `opencode-ai`, `@openai/codex`, `@github/copilot`
106	— installed globally via npm (provided by the scoop `node`). **OpenCode Desktop**
107	(GUI companion to the `opencode-ai` CLI) installs via scoop (`extras/opencode-desktop`).
108	The official **ChatGPT** and **Codex** desktop apps install via the Microsoft
109	Store (see GUI apps). SpecStory has no native-Windows CLI package, so it's
110	omitted here.
111	
112	## PowerShell modules (PSGallery)
113	
114	- **PSFzf** — fzf key bindings (Ctrl+t files, Ctrl+r history).
115	- **AudioDeviceCmdlets** — absolute volume + mute for the `sysvol`/`sysmute` helpers.
116	
117	## Opt-in dev stacks
118	
119	Off by default; enable the matching init prompt:
120	
121	| Toggle | Installs |
122	|---|---|
123	| Local LLM tools | Ollama (`Ollama.Ollama`) + LiteLLM (`uv tool install 'litellm[proxy]'`) |
124	| Tunnel tools | ngrok, cloudflared (scoop) |
125	| IaC tools | Terraform, OpenTofu (scoop) + Azure CLI & the `azure-devops` extension (`az devops`/`az repos`, winget) |
126	
127	**China mirrors** routes pip / npm / cargo / go / node package fetches through GFW
128	mirrors (Tsinghua / npmmirror / goproxy.cn / rsproxy), applied both at install time
129	and in the interactive shell (`profile.d/05_mirrors.ps1`).
130	
131	## Television (tv) channels
132	
133	Custom pickers under `%APPDATA%\television\cable\` (run `tv` to browse, or `tv <name>`):
134	
135	| Channel | What |
136	|---|---|
137	| `aliases` | PowerShell aliases & functions (cached at shell startup) |
138	| `git-ops` | common git commands → Enter copies to clipboard |
139	| `ports` | listening TCP ports → Ctrl+K kills the owning process |
140	| `kill-process` | processes by memory → Enter/Ctrl+K kill, Ctrl+D force-kill |
141	| `scoop-apps` | installed scoop apps → Enter info, Ctrl+U update, Ctrl+X uninstall |
142	| `apps` | launch an app from Start Menu shortcuts |
143	| `channels` | browse every tv channel by description → Enter opens the selected one |
144	
145	## Upgrades
146	
147	Install and upgrade are separate. `chezmoi apply` only installs what's missing;
148	upgrades are explicit:
149	
150	```powershell
151	just upgrade-scoop     # scoop update *
152	just upgrade-winget    # winget upgrade --all
153	```
154
```

 `./docs/setup.md`

```text
1	# Setup
2	
3	## One-line install
4	
5	From a fresh Windows PowerShell (or PowerShell 7) session:
6	
7	```powershell
8	irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/daviddwlee84/dotfiles-windows/main/bootstrap.ps1 | iex
9	```
10	
11	`bootstrap.ps1` does the following, idempotently:
12	
13	1. Sets the execution policy to `RemoteSigned` for the current user.
14	2. Installs [scoop](https://scoop.sh) (user-scoped, no admin — but if the shell
15	   is already elevated it auto-passes `-RunAsAdmin`, which the installer
16	   otherwise refuses with *"Running the installer as administrator is disabled
17	   by default"*).
18	3. Installs `git`, PowerShell 7 (`pwsh`), `chezmoi`, and `uv` via scoop.
19	4. Re-launches under `pwsh` if it started in Windows PowerShell 5.1.
20	5. Runs `chezmoi init --apply` against this repo.
21	
22	!!! warning "Behind the GFW"
23	    Keep a VPN on for the bootstrap — scoop downloads git / pwsh / chezmoi / uv
24	    from **GitHub releases**. The `China mirrors` option only redirects
25	    pip / npm / cargo / go / node at runtime, **not** scoop's own downloads.
26	
27	## Init prompts
28	
29	`chezmoi init` asks a few questions once (answers are stored and never re-asked):
30	
31	| Prompt | Default | Meaning |
32	|---|---|---|
33	| Role | `workstation` | `workstation` = full desktop; `minimal` = shell only |
34	| Coding agents | on (workstation) | Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Copilot CLI, SpecStory |
35	| Windows GUI apps | on (workstation) | VSCode, Cursor, Notepad++, Terminal, Alacritty, PowerToys, Raycast, Docker Desktop, Discord |
36	| Utility apps | on (workstation) | CPU-Z, GPU-Z, TreeSize, VLC, Everything, ShareX, HWiNFO |
37	| Gaming apps | off | Steam |
38	| Extra runtimes | on (workstation) | rust, go, ruby via mise (node/bun/uv are baseline) |
39	| Media CLIs | off | ffmpeg, imagemagick |
40	| Local LLM tools | off | Ollama, LiteLLM |
41	| Tunnel tools | off | ngrok, cloudflared |
42	| IaC tools | off | Azure CLI, Terraform, OpenTofu |
43	| China mirrors | off | pip / npm / cargo / go / node via GFW mirrors |
44	| Managed machine | off | skip apps org policy usually blocks (Tailscale, Grammarly) |
45	| Backup mode | `smart` | snapshot existing files before the first apply (`smart`/`full`/`off`) |
46	| PSReadLine vi mode | on | vi editing in the shell |
47	
48	Re-run the prompts later with `chezmoi init` again, or edit
49	`%USERPROFILE%\.config\chezmoi\chezmoi.toml`.
50	
51	## Day-to-day
52	
53	```powershell
54	chezmoi diff          # preview pending changes
55	chezmoi apply         # apply
56	chezmoi update        # git pull + apply
57	just upgrade-scoop     # upgrade CLI tools
58	just upgrade-winget    # upgrade GUI apps
59	```
60	
61	## Local overrides
62	
63	Drop machine-specific tweaks or secrets in
64	`~/.config/powershell/local.ps1` — it is dot-sourced last by `$PROFILE` and is
65	never managed by chezmoi.
66	
67	## The Raycast / PowerToys clash
68	
69	Raycast and PowerToys Run both default their launcher to **Alt+Space**. Pick one:
70	disable PowerToys Run (PowerToys Settings → PowerToys Run → off) or rebind its
71	hotkey. The installer prints a reminder.
72
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/rationale.md`

```text
40	
41	Why mise didn't hold up here:
42	
43	- **Shims / activate don't reliably expose global tools.** `mise activate`'s
44	  pwsh prompt-hook and the shims dir both failed to put globally-pinned tools
45	  (`bun`, `go`) on PATH in practice — `mise use -g bun` "succeeded" yet `bun`
46	  wasn't found.
47	- **Arch gaps.** On Windows-on-ARM, `mise use -g bun` records the version but
48	  ships **no windows-arm64 bun binary**, leaving a dead shim.
49	- **It fought the machine's existing node.** A pre-existing `nvm` shadowed
50	  mise's node, so npm used the wrong one.
51	
52	scoop shims live in `~/scoop/shims`, which scoop persists to the user PATH (and
53	`profile.d/00_env.ps1` also prepends) — so `node` (`nodejs-lts`), `bun`, `go`,
54	`rustup`, `ruby` are simply **on PATH**, no activation dance. **Python** is
55	uv-managed (`uv python install --default`), which also drops unversioned
56	`python`/`python3` into `~/.local/bin`.
57	
58	The tradeoff: **no per-project runtime pinning** (mise's headline feature). For a
59	Windows dev box that's a fair trade for "the tools are actually on PATH." If you
60	want per-project versions, `scoop install mise` and use it directly — nothing
61	here depends on it. The full debugging trail lives in
62	`backlog/windows-arm64-managed-machine-rough-edges.md`.
63	
64	## Terminal multiplexer: WezTerm, not tmux/zellij
65

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```lua
-- wezterm.lua — managed by chezmoi (Windows dotfiles).
--
-- WezTerm is installed as the tmux-like multiplexer (see docs/rationale.md). On
-- Windows it otherwise defaults default_prog to cmd.exe ("DOS"), so we point it at
-- PowerShell 7 and match the Alacritty / Windows Terminal font + theme.
--
-- WezTerm reads this from ~/.config/wezterm/wezterm.lua on Windows too (XDG),
-- consistent with starship / yazi. User tweaks: keep them in a separate file and
-- require() it here, or edit via `chezmoi edit`.
local wezterm = require 'wezterm'
local config = wezterm.config_builder()

-- Open pwsh, not the default cmd.exe. pwsh.exe resolves via ~/scoop/shims on PATH.
config.default_prog = { 'pwsh.exe', '-NoLogo' }

-- Same Nerd Font the other terminals use. Unlike Alacritty, WezTerm sees per-user
-- (scoop-installed) fonts, so no machine-wide install is required here.
config.font = wezterm.font('Hack Nerd Font Mono')
config.font_size = 11.0

config.color_scheme = 'One Half Dark'
config.window_background_opacity = 0.95
config.hide_tab_bar_if_only_one_tab = true
config.use_fancy_tab_bar = false
config.default_cursor_style = 'SteadyBlock'
config.scrollback_lines = 10000
config.audible_bell = 'Disabled'

return config

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```ps1
#Requires -Version 7
# enable-sshd.ps1 — install + enable the Microsoft OpenSSH Server (sshd).
#
# Single source of truth, run in two contexts:
#   - .chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_40_openssh_server.ps1.tmpl embeds this via
#     {{ include }} and runs it during `chezmoi apply` when the installSshServer
#     toggle is on;
#   - `just enable-sshd` runs it directly from an elevated pwsh (the fallback when
#     `chezmoi apply` wasn't elevated).
#
# Everything here needs admin (Windows capability, service, firewall, HKLM shell),
# so we detect elevation and print guidance instead of failing when non-elevated.
#
# WARNING: this opens an inbound listener on TCP 22 and enables a system service.
# On a corporate/managed machine, confirm it's allowed by policy first.
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'
if (Test-Path variable:PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference) { $PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference = $false }

function Info($m) { Write-Host "==> $m" -ForegroundColor Cyan }
function Test-Admin {
    try {
        $id = [Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent()
        ([Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal]$id).IsInRole([Security.Principal.WindowsBuiltInRole]::Administrator)
    } catch { $false }
}

if (-not (Test-Admin)) {
    Write-Warning 'OpenSSH server: needs an elevated shell. Run `just enable-sshd` from an admin pwsh, or re-run `chezmoi apply` elevated.'
    return
}

try {
    # 1. Install the OpenSSH.Server Windows capability if not already present.
    $cap = Get-WindowsCapability -Online -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
        Where-Object { $_.Name -like 'OpenSSH.Server*' } | Select-Object -First 1
    if (-not $cap) {
        Write-Warning 'OpenSSH.Server capability not offered by this Windows image — install it manually (winget Microsoft.OpenSSH.Beta) then re-run.'
        return
    }
    if ($cap.State -ne 'Installed') {
        Info "Add-WindowsCapability $($cap.Name)"
        Add-WindowsCapability -Online -Name $cap.Name | Out-Null
    } else {
        Info 'OpenSSH.Server capability already installed'
    }

    # 2. Service: automatic startup + running.
    Set-Service -Name sshd -StartupType Automatic -ErrorAction Stop
    Start-Service -Name sshd -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    Info "sshd: $((Get-Service sshd).Status), start type Automatic"

    # 3. Firewall: allow inbound TCP 22. The capability usually adds this rule;
    #    recreate it if it's missing.
    if (-not (Get-NetFirewallRule -Name 'OpenSSH-Server-In-TCP' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) {
        Info 'New-NetFirewallRule OpenSSH-Server-In-TCP (inbound TCP 22)'
        New-NetFirewallRule -Name 'OpenSSH-Server-In-TCP' -DisplayName 'OpenSSH Server (sshd)' `
            -Enabled True -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -Action Allow -LocalPort 22 | Out-Null
    }

    # 4. Default shell -> pwsh, so `ssh` sessions land in PowerShell 7, not cmd.exe.
    $pwsh = (Get-Command pwsh -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Source
    if ($pwsh) {
        New-Item -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\OpenSSH' -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Out-Null
        New-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\OpenSSH' -Name DefaultShell -Value $pwsh `
            -PropertyType String -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Out-Null
        Info "sshd DefaultShell -> $pwsh"
    }

    Info 'OpenSSH server ready. From another host: ssh <user>@<this-machine-name>'
} catch {
    Write-Warning "OpenSSH server setup failed (non-fatal): $_"
}

```

```text
1	# 安裝
2	
3	## 一行安裝
4	
5	在全新的 Windows PowerShell（或 PowerShell 7）視窗執行：
6	
7	```powershell
8	irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/daviddwlee84/dotfiles-windows/main/bootstrap.ps1 | iex
9	```
10	
11	`bootstrap.ps1` 會依序（且可重複執行）完成：
12	
13	1. 將目前使用者的 execution policy 設為 `RemoteSigned`。
14	2. 安裝 [scoop](https://scoop.sh)（使用者層級、免系統管理員 —— 但若 shell 本身是
15	   系統管理員身分，會自動帶上 `-RunAsAdmin`；否則安裝程式會以
16	   *「Running the installer as administrator is disabled by default」* 拒絕執行）。
17	3. 透過 scoop 安裝 `git`、PowerShell 7（`pwsh`）、`chezmoi`、`uv`。
18	4. 若是從 Windows PowerShell 5.1 啟動，會改用 `pwsh` 重新執行。
19	5. 對此 repo 執行 `chezmoi init --apply`。
20	
21	!!! warning "在 GFW 後面"
22	    bootstrap 期間請開著 VPN —— scoop 會從 **GitHub releases** 下載
23	    git / pwsh / chezmoi / uv。`China mirrors` 選項只在執行期重導
24	    pip / npm / cargo / go / node，**不涵蓋** scoop 自己的下載。
25	
26	## 初始化提問
27	
28	`chezmoi init` 只會問一次（答案會被記住，不再重複問）：
29	
30	| 提問 | 預設 | 意義 |
31	|---|---|---|
32	| Role | `workstation` | `workstation` = 完整桌面；`minimal` = 只有 shell |
33	| Coding agents | 開（workstation） | Claude Code、OpenCode、Codex、Copilot CLI、SpecStory |
34	| Windows GUI apps | 開（workstation） | VSCode、Cursor、Notepad++、Terminal、Alacritty、PowerToys、Raycast、Docker Desktop、Discord |
35	| Utility apps | 開（workstation） | CPU-Z、GPU-Z、TreeSize、VLC、Everything、ShareX、HWiNFO |
36	| Gaming apps | 關 | Steam |
37	| Extra runtimes | 開（workstation） | 透過 mise 安裝 rust、go、ruby（node/bun/uv 為基本內建） |
38	| Media CLIs | 關 | ffmpeg、imagemagick |
39	| Local LLM tools | 關 | Ollama、LiteLLM |
40	| Tunnel tools | 關 | ngrok、cloudflared |
41	| IaC tools | 關 | Azure CLI、Terraform、OpenTofu |
42	| China mirrors | 關 | pip / npm / cargo / go / node 走 GFW 鏡像 |
43	| Managed machine | 關 | 略過 org 政策常擋的 app（Tailscale、Grammarly） |
44	| Backup mode | `smart` | 首次 apply 前備份既有檔案（`smart`/`full`/`off`） |
45	| PSReadLine vi mode | 開 | shell 的 vi 編輯模式 |
46	
47	之後可再次執行 `chezmoi init` 重新提問，或直接編輯
48	`%USERPROFILE%\.config\chezmoi\chezmoi.toml`。
49	
50	## 日常操作
51	
52	```powershell
53	chezmoi diff          # 預覽即將套用的變更
54	chezmoi apply         # 套用
55	chezmoi update        # git pull 後套用
56	just upgrade-scoop     # 升級 CLI 工具
57	just upgrade-winget    # 升級 GUI 應用程式
58	```
59	
60	## 本機覆寫
61	
62	把機器專屬的調整或機密放在 `~/.config/powershell/local.ps1` —— 它會被 `$PROFILE`
63	最後 dot-source，且永遠不受 chezmoi 管理。
64	
65	## Raycast 與 PowerToys 的衝突
66	
67	Raycast 與 PowerToys Run 的啟動器預設都用 **Alt+Space**。請擇一：關閉 PowerToys Run
68	（PowerToys 設定 → PowerToys Run → 關）或改綁它的快捷鍵。安裝腳本會印出提醒。
69
```
