 `./.chezmoitemplates/dotfiles-windows-skill.md`

```text
1	---
2	name: dotfiles-windows
3	description: Operate and navigate THIS Windows machine's chezmoi-managed dotfiles (native PowerShell 7). Use when the user asks how to apply/edit/diff dotfiles, where a config or tool lives, how the pwsh $PROFILE / copilot-proxy / scoop+winget install works, how a keybinding or helper (applaunch/sysvol/x/cas) works, or how to use chezmoi / just in this repo.
4	---
5	
6	# dotfiles-windows
7	
8	This machine's `$HOME` is managed by a **chezmoi** dotfiles repo targeting **native
9	Windows + PowerShell 7**. Packages install via **scoop** (CLI) + **winget** (GUI) —
10	there is no ansible here. Repo: <https://github.com/daviddwlee84/dotfiles-windows>.
11	(macOS/Linux/WSL are handled by the separate `daviddwlee84/dotfiles` repo.)
12	
13	## Orientation
14	- Source dir: `chezmoi source-path` — edit there (`cd "$(chezmoi source-path)"`).
15	- Preview / apply: `chezmoi diff` / `chezmoi apply` (or `just diff` / `just apply`).
16	- Source of a target: `chezmoi source-path <path>`; render without applying: `chezmoi cat <path>`.
17	- Re-run init prompts: `chezmoi init` (answers persist in `~/.config/chezmoi/chezmoi.toml`).
18	- `docs/**`, `editors/**`, `scripts/**` are chezmoi-ignored (not deployed).
19	
20	## Shell layout (pwsh)
21	- `$PROFILE` (`~/Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1`) is a loader
22	  that dot-sources `~/.config/powershell/profile.d/*.ps1` in sorted order, then
23	  `~/.config/powershell/local.ps1` (untracked user override, loaded last).
24	- Fragments: `00_env` (PATH/XDG/env), `10_tools` (starship, zoxide, atuin,
25	  fzf, direnv, tv), `20_aliases` (`ll`/`gs`/`reload`/`cas`/`cau`/`chezmoi-cd`/`run-for`),
26	  `30_apps` (`applaunch`/`appquit`/`apprestart`/`sysvol`/`sysmute`/`x`), `35_yazi` (`y`),
27	  `40_copilot` (imports the Copilot module), `90_psreadline` (vi-mode gated on `enableVimMode`).
28	
29	## Packages
30	- `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl` is the **single source of
31	  truth** for installs, gated by the init toggles. scoop = CLI tools, winget = GUI
32	  apps, npm = AI agents, PSGallery = PSFzf/AudioDeviceCmdlets. Fault-tolerant: one
33	  failed package is collected + reported, never aborts the apply.
34	- Install ≠ upgrade: `chezmoi apply` only installs what's missing; upgrade via
35	  `just upgrade-scoop` / `just upgrade-winget`.
36	
37	## copilot-proxy
38	- Module at `~/.config/powershell/modules/Copilot`. Commands: `copilot-proxy`
39	  (`start`/`stop`/`restart`/`status`/`doctor`/`logs`/`shim`/`whoami`/`auth`),
40	  `copilot-run`, `claude-copilot`, `claude-copilot-once`, `copilot-here`,
41	  `copilot-model`, `copilot-embed`, `semsearch`.
42	- Needs `bun`. Token: `~/.local/share/copilot-api/github_token`. Ports 4141 (proxy) /
43	  4142 (throttle shim). Default model `claude-opus-4-8[1m]`. `copilot-here` writes
44	  only the gitignored `./.claude/settings.local.json`. Full guide: `docs/copilot-proxy.md`.
45	
46	## Editors / terminal / tv
47	- VSCode & Cursor settings + keybindings are deep-merged into `%APPDATA%\{Code,Cursor}\User`
48	  by `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_20_editor_overlays.ps1.tmpl` (sources under `editors/`).
49	- Windows Terminal `profiles.defaults` and Alacritty (`%APPDATA%\alacritty\alacritty.toml`)
50	  are managed too. Television channels live under `%APPDATA%\television\cable\` (`tv <name>`).
51	
52	## What's enabled on THIS machine
53	- role: **{{ .role }}**
54	- Coding agents: {{ .installCodingAgents }} · GUI apps: {{ .installWindowsApps }} · Utility apps: {{ .installUtilityApps }} · Gaming: {{ .installGamingApps }}
55	- Extra runtimes: {{ .installExtraRuntimes }} · Media: {{ .installMediaTools }} · LLM: {{ .installLlmTools }} · Tunnel: {{ .installTunnelTools }} · IaC: {{ .installIacTools }}
56	- China mirrors: {{ .useChineseMirror }} · Backup mode: {{ .backupMode }} · Vim mode: {{ .enableVimMode }} · Managed machine: {{ .managedMachine }}
57	
58	## just recipes
59	`just --list`: `apply`/`diff`/`update`, `upgrade-scoop`/`upgrade-winget`, `lint`/`test`,
60	`docs-serve`/`docs-build`, and `docker-up`/`docker-down`/`docker-clean`/`docker-logs`
61	(the x86-Linux+KVM Windows-in-Docker test harness — see `docker/windows/`).
62	
63	## Gotchas
64	- Windows-only repo — no `{{ "{{" }} if eq .chezmoi.os {{ "}}" }}` branching needed.
65	- Editor settings use a `run_onchange` pwsh merger (not `modify_`) on Windows.
66	- tmux / zellij are Unix-only and intentionally absent; **WezTerm** (installed) is
67	  the native tmux-like multiplexer, or use Windows Terminal panes. Runtimes are
68	  native via scoop (node/bun/go/rust/ruby) + uv for Python — no mise on Windows.
69	- This skill body is shared: `dot_agents/skills/dotfiles-windows/` and
70	  `dot_claude/skills/dotfiles-windows/` both render `.chezmoitemplates/dotfiles-windows-skill.md`.
71
```
</details></tool-use>

#Requires -Version 7

{{ end -}}

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```ps1
#Requires -Version 7
# install-fonts-machine-wide.ps1 — install the Hack Nerd Fonts machine-wide via
# scoop global (needs admin).
#
# Fixes Alacritty on Windows not finding a Nerd Font that scoop installed per-user:
# Alacritty enumerates only the machine-wide (HKLM) font collection, so a scoop
# `nerd-fonts` install (registered per-user in HKCU) is invisible to it — while
# Windows Terminal and WezTerm see per-user fonts fine. `scoop install -g` installs
# a global copy that registers machine-wide.
#
# Run via `just install-fonts-machine-wide` from an ELEVATED pwsh.
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'

function Test-Admin {
    try {
        $id = [Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent()
        ([Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal]$id).IsInRole([Security.Principal.WindowsBuiltInRole]::Administrator)
    } catch { $false }
}

if (-not (Test-Admin)) {
    Write-Warning 'Machine-wide font install needs an elevated shell. Run `just install-fonts-machine-wide` from an admin pwsh.'
    return
}
if (-not (Get-Command scoop -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) {
    Write-Warning 'scoop not found on PATH.'
    return
}

scoop bucket add nerd-fonts *> $null
Write-Host '==> scoop install -g Hack-NF-Mono Hack-NF (machine-wide)' -ForegroundColor Cyan
scoop install -g Hack-NF-Mono Hack-NF
Write-Host '==> Done. Restart Alacritty to pick up the machine-wide font.' -ForegroundColor Green

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pitfalls/README.md`

```text
1	# Pitfalls
2	
3	Past traps we've stepped on. **Symptoms-first** knowledge base — when a
4	problem recurs (on a new machine, after an upgrade, with a new tool combo),
5	grepping the symptom here lands you on the root cause and workaround in
6	seconds, instead of re-debugging from scratch.
7	
8	This folder is excluded from <DEPLOYMENT/PACKAGING MECHANISM, e.g. chezmoi via
9	.chezmoiignore.tmpl, Python via MANIFEST.in, npm via .npmignore> — it is repo
10	metadata for maintainers, not user-facing config to deploy.
11	
12	## Pitfalls vs the rest
13	
14	| Surface | Time direction | Question it answers | Access pattern |
15	|---|---|---|---|
16	| `docs/<tool>.md` | Present | "How does this tool work / how do I configure it?" | Read top to bottom |
17	| `pitfalls/<slug>.md` | **Past** | **"I see error X — has this happened before?"** | **Grep symptoms** |
18	| `backlog/<slug>.md` | Future | "We thought about doing Y — what was the analysis?" | Index in `TODO.md` |
19	| `AGENTS.md` Hard invariants | Present | "What rules MUST agents follow?" | Read top to bottom |
20	
21	A pitfall **graduates** to a Hard invariant when the trap is serious enough
22	that you can't rely on memory or grep — typically when (a) it recurs across
23	machines, (b) it silently corrupts state, or (c) the workaround is non-obvious
24	and easy to undo by accident. When graduating, leave a `pitfalls/<slug>.md`
25	as historical record and link to it from the new invariant.
26	
27	## When to add a pitfall doc
28	
29	Add `pitfalls/<slug>.md` when you've spent more than ~15 minutes on something
30	that wasn't googleable, AND any of:
31	
32	- The symptom is non-obvious from the root cause (silent state, weird side
33	  effect, behaviour change without error)
34	- The fix is "do nothing different but in a specific order"
35	- The same trap could be hit by a new agent / new machine / new contributor
36	- An upstream bug exists with no ETA — workaround needs to outlive memory
37	- A specific tool version is required (or forbidden) and failure at the
38	  wrong version is silent / confusing
39	
40	## When NOT to add a pitfall doc
41	
42	- Trivially googleable (next person solves in 30 seconds)
43	- Already covered in `docs/<tool>.md` — cross-link from this README's
44	  "Cross-referenced pitfalls" table below instead of duplicating
45	- Already a Hard invariant (cross-link only)
46	- One-off transient (network glitch, machine-specific config rot)
47	
48	## File template
49	
50	See [`pitfall-doc.md.template`] in the
51	[`project-knowledge-harness` skill](https://github.com/daviddwlee84/agent-skills/tree/main/skills/local/project-knowledge-harness)
52	for the per-doc template.
53	
54	Key sections (different from `backlog/` template — symptom-first, not
55	context-first):
56	
57	```markdown
58	# <Title describing the SYMPTOM, not the root cause>
59	
60	**Symptoms** (grep this section): <verbatim error messages, observable behaviour>
61	**First seen**: YYYY-MM
62	**Affects**: <tool/version/OS combo>
63	**Status**: workaround documented / fixed upstream in vX.Y / WONTFIX
64	
65	## Symptom
66	
67	Full error messages (verbatim — preserves grep-ability).
68	
69	## Root cause
70	
71	Why this happens, with source/docs/upstream issue link.
72	
73	## Workaround
74	
75	Copy-pasteable commands or config diff.
76	
77	## Prevention
78	
79	How to avoid stepping on this again.
80	
81	## Related
82	
83	Links to docs, sibling pitfalls, TODO entries, upstream issues.
84	```
85	
86	## Index
87	
88	Pitfalls owned by this folder. Keep alphabetical.
89	
90	| Slug | Symptom keywords | Status |
91	|---|---|---|
92	| (none yet) | | |
93	
94	## Cross-referenced pitfalls (still in their original homes)
95	
96	These traps are documented elsewhere and aren't duplicated here — the table
97	exists so grepping `pitfalls/` still finds them. Move into this folder only
98	if their original location stops being a natural reading flow.
99	
100	| Trap | Lives in | Why not here |
101	|---|---|---|
102	| (example: Tool X version Y bug) | `docs/tool-x.md` → "Known issues" | Already part of the tool's normal config narrative |
103
```
</details></tool-use>

Render all templates then parse-check in a single pwsh pass

```bash
cd /Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-windows-dotfiles
TMPD=$(mktemp -d); mkdir -p "$TMPD/home" "$TMPD/rendered"
chezmoi init --source="$PWD" --config="$TMPD/c.toml" --persistent-state="$TMPD/s.db" --destination="$TMPD/home" --no-tty \
  --promptChoice='Role: workstation (full desktop) or minimal (shell only)=minimal' \
  --promptString='Your full name (git)=T' --promptString='Your git email=t@e.com' \
  --promptBool='Install coding agents (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Copilot CLI, SpecStory)=false' \
  --promptBool='Install GUI apps via winget (VSCode, Cursor, Notepad++, Terminal, Alacritty, PowerToys, Raycast)=false' \
  --promptBool='Install utility apps via winget (CPU-Z, GPU-Z, TreeSize, VLC, Everything, ShareX, HWiNFO)=false' \
  --promptBool='Install gaming apps (Steam)=false' \
  --promptBool='Install extra runtimes (rust, go, ruby) beyond node/bun/uv=false' \
  --promptBool='Install media CLIs (ffmpeg, imagemagick)=false' \
  --promptBool='Install local LLM tools (Ollama, LiteLLM)=false' \
  --promptBool='Install tunnel tools (ngrok, cloudflared)=false' \
  --promptBool='Install Infrastructure-as-Code tools (Azure CLI, Terraform, OpenTofu)=false' \
  --promptBool='Install and enable the OpenSSH server / sshd (needs admin, opens inbound port 22)=true' \
  --promptBool='Use China (GFW) mirrors for pip/npm/cargo/go/node=false' \
  --promptBool='Managed or corporate machine (skip org-policy-blocked apps like Tailscale and Grammarly)=false' \
  --promptChoice='Back up existing files before apply (smart|full|off)=off' \
  --promptBool='Enable PSReadLine vi editing mode=true' >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "init OK"
# Render every .ps1.tmpl (chezmoi only — no pwsh, no execution)
renderfail=0
for f in $(find . -name '*.ps1.tmpl' -not -path './.git/*' | sort); do
  out="$TMPD/rendered/$(echo "$f" | tr '/.' '__').ps1"
  chezmoi execute-template --config="$TMPD/c.toml" --source="$PWD" < "$f" > "$out" 2>"$TMPD/e.txt" \
    && { [ -s "$out" ] && echo "render OK   $f" || { echo "EMPTY: $f"; renderfail=1; }; } \
    || { echo "RENDER FAIL: $f"; cat "$TMPD/e.txt"; renderfail=1; }
done
# Copy plain .ps1 (profile.d + scripts) for parsing too
for f in $(find dot_config/powershell/profile.d scripts -name '*.ps1' -not -name '*.tmpl' | sort); do
  cp "$f" "$TMPD/rendered/plain__$(echo "$f" | tr '/.' '__').ps1"
done
echo "renderfail=$renderfail"
echo "=== single pwsh pass: ParseFile each (no execution) ==="
CHEZ_DIR="$TMPD/rendered" pwsh -NoProfile -Command '
  $bad = 0
  Get-ChildItem -Path $env:CHEZ_DIR -Filter *.ps1 | Sort-Object Name | ForEach-Object {
    $e = $null
    [System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile($_.FullName, [ref]$null, [ref]$e) | Out-Null
    if ($e) { Write-Host ("PARSE FAIL: " + $_.Name + " (" + $e.Count + ")"); $e | ForEach-Object { Write-Host "   $_" }; $bad++ }
    else { Write-Host ("OK:   " + $_.Name) }
  }
  Write-Host ("PARSE RESULT: " + ($(if($bad -eq 0){"ALL-GREEN"}else{"$bad FILE(S) FAILED"})))
  if ($bad) { exit 3 }
'
echo "pwsh-exit=$?"
echo "(side-effect check) ~/scoop: $(ls -d ~/scoop 2>/dev/null || echo absent) ; ~/.config/powershell: $(ls -d ~/.config/powershell 2>/dev/null || echo absent)"
rm -rf "$TMPD"
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>

```md
# pwsh $PROFILE not loaded / "is not recognized" under OneDrive Known Folder Move

**Symptoms** (grep this section): `is not recognized as a name of a cmdlet, function, script file, or executable`; `Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1`; a bare `PS C:\...>` prompt with no starship / no aliases; the error path shows `OneDrive - <tenant>\Documents\PowerShell`.
**First seen**: 2026-07
**Affects**: Windows with OneDrive "Known Folder Move" (Documents redirected) — common on corporate/managed machines; chezmoi-managed pwsh `$PROFILE`.
**Status**: fixed — `run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1.tmpl` now writes the loader **content** (previously a dot-source pointer stub).

## Symptom

A new pwsh session errors on startup, then falls back to a bare prompt:

\```
. : C:\Users\<user>\OneDrive - <tenant>\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1:4
Line |
   4 |  . "C:\Users\<user>\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profi …
     |    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
     | The term 'C:\Users\<user>\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1'
     | is not recognized as a name of a cmdlet, function, script file, or executable program.
\```

No starship prompt, no aliases — the managed profile never loaded.

## Root cause

chezmoi writes the managed loader to the **literal** `~/Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1` (it joins `Documents` off `%USERPROFILE%`, unaware of shell Known Folders). But when OneDrive **Known Folder Move** redirects Documents, interactive pwsh resolves `$PROFILE` to `...\OneDrive - <tenant>\Documents\PowerShell\...` — a *different* path.

The old redirect script bridged this by writing a **stub** at the real `$PROFILE` that dot-sourced the literal path:

\```powershell
. "C:\Users\<user>\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1"
\```

Under KFM the literal `~/Documents` copy is **not guaranteed to exist** at `$PROFILE`-load time (OneDrive may have moved/removed it), so the dot-source target is missing → "is not recognized". The stub itself was written fine; only the thing it pointed at was gone. (This is pure path topology — not a privilege issue.)

## Workaround

Fixed in-repo: the redirect script now writes the managed loader's **content** into `$PROFILE`. The loader is location-independent (it only sources `~/.config/powershell`), so there is nothing left to point at. To recover a broken machine by hand:

\```powershell
chezmoi cat "$HOME\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1" |
    Set-Content -LiteralPath $PROFILE -Encoding utf8
. $PROFILE
\```

`chezmoi cat` renders the managed file from source without needing the literal copy on disk. A following `chezmoi apply` re-runs the redirect script to keep the OneDrive copy in sync.

## Prevention

- The redirect writes **content, not a pointer**, so a missing literal `~/Documents` copy can no longer break `$PROFILE` load, and there's no self-source recursion risk.
- The loader is embedded via `{{ include }}`, so `run_onchange` re-fires whenever the loader changes and the OneDrive copy stays current (the old plain-`.ps1` stub never updated on loader edits).

## Related

- `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_02_profile_redirect.ps1.tmpl`
- `Documents/PowerShell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1` (the loader)
- Graduate to an `AGENTS.md` Hard invariant if it recurs across machines (see `pitfalls/README.md`).

```
